<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015309 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: (2026x Refresh1) CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

- page_id: `304015309`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015309/2026x+Refresh1+CATIA+Magic+Cameo+SysML+v2+Community+Edition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-04T08:23:31.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With the Object Management Group (OMG) releasing **SysML v2**, the community is eager to explore and learn this new language. To support this journey, our teams are introducing **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition**– a game-changer for anyone looking to master**SysML v2**!

- 100% Standard Conformance
  - Full adherence to the OMG SysML v2 specification metamodel.
- Textual and Graphical Syntax in Perfect Sync
  - Instant two-way synchronization between textual and graphical representations.
- Designed for Learning and Training
  - Model up to 500 major elements - ideal for practice and language exploration.
- Free of Charge

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Downloading the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

To download the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

1. Go to https://software.3ds.com/
2. Create an account (if you don't have one yet) and sign in.
3. Click Free Download > CATIA Magic/No Magic .
4. Find CATIA Magic SysML v2 Community Edition in the list.
5. Click 1/5 for Windows, 2/5 for Mac, 3/5 for Linux, 4/5 for Windows no install, or 5/5 for Mac aarch. [ATTACHMENT filename='download_community_ed.png']
6. Install the product you just downloaded (Magic Systems of Systems Architect, or MSOSA for short).
7. Launch the Magic Systems of Systems Architect.
8. In the License Manager, select Use in Community Mode . [ATTACHMENT filename='License Manager.png']
9. Read the license agreement and click I agree .
10. Once the Welcome screen loads, you will see a prompt indicating that the SysML v2 plugin must be installed.
11. Click the Install button in the prompt. The Resource Manager opens with the SysML v2 plugin already selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resource Manager.png']
12. In the Resource Manager , click the Download/Install button to install the SysML v2 plugin.
13. Restart the tool. You are now running the modeling tool in SysML v2 Community Edition Mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">With the Object Management Group (OMG) releasing <strong>SysML v2</strong>, the community is eager to explore and learn this new language. To support this journey, our teams are introducing <strong>CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition</strong><span> </span>– a game-changer for anyone looking to master<span> </span><strong>SysML v2</strong>!</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="a4f7fcb1-596b-4559-9c06-8f72cb824f95">100% Standard Conformance<ul><li>Full adherence to the OMG SysML v2 specification metamodel.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="3ca762c0-c780-4c6c-a434-26feb91e90b2">Textual and Graphical Syntax in Perfect Sync<ul><li>Instant two-way synchronization between textual and graphical representations.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="ef012ea1-dfb5-4735-906b-629c216d3f28">Designed for Learning and Training<ul><li>Model up to 500 major elements - ideal for practice and language exploration.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="38d43374-8b05-4f7a-8e16-0e961e727949">Free of Charge</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CATIA MagicCameo SysML v2 Community Edition.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Downloading the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition</h3><p>To download the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1e42257c-2872-47f1-8862-e1bffde0391e">Go to <a href="https://software.3ds.com/">https://software.3ds.com/</a></li><li data-uuid="df786d36-7070-48c9-979b-9f2fd27dd6ed">Create an account (if you don't have one yet) and sign in.</li><li data-uuid="d1c7f7eb-d9f7-490f-b99f-af4e1fd7cc05">Click <strong>Free Download </strong>&gt; <strong>CATIA Magic/No Magic</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="49599e7c-3291-4dd7-b239-ea2dc5d90207">Find <strong>CATIA Magic SysML v2 Community Edition</strong> in the list.</li><li data-uuid="1c55a90e-6b79-4079-bd43-87537a6cf79e">Click <strong>1/5</strong> for Windows, <strong>2/5</strong> for Mac, <strong>3/5</strong> for Linux, <strong>4/5</strong> for Windows no install, or <strong>5/5</strong> for Mac aarch. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="download_community_ed.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="a07e2bfe-25e9-48ed-9ca6-0a42fe8986b8">Install the product you just downloaded (Magic Systems of Systems Architect, or MSOSA for short).</li><li data-uuid="148ade23-e3a8-44f3-b1a8-ec7e7ad506df">Launch the Magic Systems of Systems Architect.</li><li data-uuid="8f2c1d01-08c6-4de1-b808-26c857407a94">In the License Manager, select <strong>Use in Community Mode</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="License Manager.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="04aa2913-bb5b-44c6-b7f8-be3c04bec1c9">Read the license agreement and click <strong>I agree</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="21983335-f86f-4202-ad64-41adac795a3a">Once the Welcome screen loads, you will see a prompt indicating that the SysML v2 plugin must be installed.</li><li data-uuid="660ed856-393c-47c3-a585-4746b4776ab1">Click the <strong>Install</strong> button in the prompt. The <strong>Resource Manager</strong> opens with the SysML v2 plugin already selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Resource Manager.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="79e49ef7-4fc2-4ea3-873c-759bfa5b5675">In the <strong>Resource Manager</strong>, click the <strong>Download/Install </strong>button to install the SysML v2 plugin.</li><li data-uuid="1de2c8c3-1810-4e8d-a533-7d7693400e0c">Restart the tool. You are now running the modeling tool in SysML v2 Community Edition Mode.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00027: (2026x Refresh1) CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Solution

- page_id: `312246433`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### THE MOST POWERFUL, INTEGRATED, AND 100% STANDARD-COMPLIANT SYSML V2 SOLUTION

We invite you to watch the video from our September 2025 webinar showcasing our SysML v2 solution overview.

400249

##### CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition: For Learning and Teaching - Completely Free

With the Object Management Group (OMG) releasing **SysML v2**, the community is eager to explore and learn this new language. To support this journey, our teams are introducing **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition** – a game-changer for anyone looking to master **SysML v2**!

- 100% Standard Conformance
  - Full adherence to the OMG SysML v2 specification metamodel.
- Textual and Graphical Syntax in Perfect Sync
  - Instant two-way synchronization between textual and graphical representations.
- Designed for Learning and Training
  - Model up to 500 major elements - ideal for practice and language exploration.
- Free of Charge

For download instructions, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### SysML v2 Quick Sheet

For your convenience, the Dassault Systèmes and OOSE co-authored SysML v2 quick sheet is available for download below as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language using our SysML v2 solution.

###### [ATTACHMENT filename='2025-10 SysMLv2 Cheatsheet.pdf'][IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Click the image, then click Download in the top-right corner to download the full file.

##### Standard Conformance

Full compliance with the SysML v2 standard is at the core of our implementation.

- Built on principle – our team makes standard compliance a core value; every engineer is committed to it.
- Interoperability – models exchange seamlessly with other tools following the standard.
- Ecosystem-ready – full conformance simplifies integration across the partner ecosystem.
- Future-proofing – no risk of vendor lock-in or compatibility gaps as the standard evolves.
- Trust & Transparency – engineers know their models are pure SysML v2, not tool-specific variants.

By delivering 100% standard conformance, Dassault Systèmes ensures that customers benefit from the**full power of SysML v2.**

##### Text and Diagrams in Perfect Sync

CATIA Magic/Cameo products deliver**seamless two-way synchronization**between**textual**and**graphical**SysML v2 model representations.

- Instant consistency – changes in text or diagrams are immediately reflected in both views.
- Dual approach – textual syntax appeals to software developers, while diagrams provide clarity for complex system architectures.
- Unique capability – currently the only solution supporting 100% of the SysML v2 standard with true two-way synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Dynamic Views

SysML v2 supports**three model visualization options**:

- Graphical
- Tabular
- Textual

In CATIA Magic/Cameo, users can**dynamically generate graphical and tabular views**by specifying**expressions that query the model**.

- Automatic updates – views can be rebuilt automatically when the model changes, saving systems engineers significant time and effort.
- Reusable definitions – view definitions can be stored in libraries and reused across projects and teams.
- Efficient modeling – eliminates manual adjustments.

Dynamic views are one of the most powerful capabilities in CATIA Magic/Cameo, enabling**efficient, consistent, and reusable visualizations**throughout the organization.

400249

400249 400249

##### Model Evaluation

SysML v2 introduces a powerful expression language for concise navigation and analysis of models. Using dot‑notation for feature chaining and collection operators such as sum(), complex queries can be written in a compact, intuitive form. Expressions are evaluated in full model context, honoring inheritance, redefinitions, subsets, and library functions.

CATIA Magic/Cameo enhances this with features that make analysis seamless:

- Requirements verification – any requirement violations are immediately flagged, providing instant traceability to violated constraints.
- Roll-ups & trade studies – apply roll-up patterns, perform “what-if” analyses, and conduct trade-off studies directly on the model.
- Evaluation in Model or Console – run expressions directly within the model or use the Evaluation Console to execute custom expressions without modifying the model.
- Advanced evaluation – supports analysis at both model and meta-model levels.
- Interactive diagrams – adjust initial values to test system behavior without modifying the model.

This combination of language and product capabilities**bridges modeling and analysis**, enabling**data-driven decision-making**and**full traceability from design exploration to validation**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

400249

##### **Model Simulation**

Bridge the gap between static design and dynamic validation with the **SysML v2 Simulation** that transforms structural architectures into **executable, verifiable system representations** early in the lifecycle.

CATIA Magic/Cameo delivers a robust execution environment designed to make analysis seamless:

- State Machine Execution - execute complex state machine logic alongside structural connectivity to verify state-based behaviors and system interactions before prototyping.
- Live Visual Verification - track execution paths in real time with intuitive model highlights , dynamic runtime value updates, and automatic requirements verification .
- Interactive Debugging - change initial values mid-execution, manually fire transitions, or inject triggers to rapidly analyze edge cases and unexpected operational scenarios
- Time-Series Plotting & Data Export – monitor complex numerical variables over time using the Time Series Chart , or export complete execution datasets to CSV for downstream verification and analysis.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Model Validation**

Maintaining model quality is essential for ensuring consistency, correctness, and compliance with modeling standards. CATIA Magic/Cameo introduces comprehensive model validation capabilities that automatically evaluate models against validation rules provided by SysML, KerML, and Dassault Systèmes predefined validation suites.

CATIA Magic/Cameo provides multiple ways to validate and review model content:

- Validation in views – failing elements are highlighted directly in views, enabling immediate identification of issues. Detailed validation results can be accessed through element smart manipulators, while the Validation Results panel provides a consolidated overview of all failures within the current view.
- Validation in the Textual Editor – textual notation is validated automatically as you work. Failing elements are underlined, validation details are available on hover, allowing rapid issue identification without leaving the editor.
- Specific scope validation – validate specific scope together with all recursively contained elements using the Validate command, available from both the Containment tree.
- Custom validation rules – create model-based validation suites and rules to enforce organization specific modeling standards.
- Validation suite management – enable or disable predefined and custom validation suites through project settings, providing flexibility to align validation behavior with project requirements.

By integrating validation directly into graphical, tabular, and textual modeling workflows, CATIA Magic/Cameo helps teams detect issues earlier, maintain higher model quality, and ensure adherence to modeling standards throughout the development lifecycle.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

400249

##### Adopt SysML v2 at Your Own Pace

Organizations can adopt SysML v2 at their own pace thanks tothe**full co-existence of SysML v2 with SysML v1**. Both versions can be authored in the same CATIA Magic/Cameo modeling environment, with the ability to select SysML v1 or v2 per project. Migration is supported by the SysML v2 transformation specification, ensuring legacy models remain usable and protecting existing investments.

Key features:

- Authoring – co-existence of SysML v1 and v2 in the same environment
  - Choose v1 or v2 on a per-project basis
  - Support for SysML v2 transformation specifications for smooth migration, including diagrams
- API – server REST API continuity
  - Full SysML v2 standard API support
  - Continue using existing server REST API for both v1 and v2 projects
- Simulation – use case continuity from v1
  - Core SysML v1 simulation use cases supported in SysML v2
- User experience – focus on usability
  - Best features from SysML v1 implementation reused to maximize user efficiency

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

400249****

##### Standard SysML v2 REST API: CI/CD workflows, automation, querying, model evaluation

Teamwork Cloud fully conforms to the**SysML v2 API and Services**specification, ensuring interoperability with external tools and deployment of automated MBSE workflows.

- Programmatic access – create, edit, query, retrieve, and evaluate models directly from scripts or applications.
- Seamless integration – connect with data analytics platforms, DevOps CI/CD pipelines, and custom engineering workflows.
- Custom API extensions – query and analyze SysML v2 model more efficiently with CATIA Magic's home-grown Model Evaluation services. Stay tuned for more exciting API extensions!

This combination enables engineers to effectively**navigate, query, and analyze SysML v2 models**with full standard compliance and interoperability in mind.

##### Collaborative Modeling Through Teamwork Cloud

**Teamwork Cloud (TWC)** enables secure, lock-free collaborative modeling for SysML v2.

- Simultaneous collaboration – multiple users can work on the same model at the same time.
- Automatic merging – non-conflicting changes are merged automatically; intuitive resolution for overlaps.
- Versioned & modular models – decomposition into manageable units, supporting efficient collaboration across teams, organizations, and geographies. Supports collaborative development in branches for experimentation, with reintegration through a model patch mechanism.
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) – fine-grained permissions at the project or branch level to protect data access, ensuring that users and user groups have appropriate access rights.
- Support for U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) requirements
  - Provides support for Data Markings/Classification based on the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification, allowing to mark security-sensitive data such as users and projects with custom-defined data markings
  - Data spillage handling via dedicated tools for physical removal of model data affected by data spillage incidents
  - On-premise deployment for isolated classified programs
  - Prime-sub model exchange workflows built with scalability and IP protection in mind
- Standards-based SAML authentication integrations – supports single sign-on including built-in, LDAP, PKI/CAC, and SAML-based authentication.
- Auditing – provides access to audit reports to track user access and permissions granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.
- Cross-cluster synchronization – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.

Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).

400249

##### MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)

MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**MagicLab Collaborator**

Introducing MagicLab Collaborator, a service that enables teams to collaborate on SysML v2 projects. Building on the capabilities of MagicLab, which is a read-only viewer, MagicLab Collaborator includes flexible collaboration features such as branch-specific feedback, commenting with search and editing, and support for working across multiple branches. Users can also open and review both current and historical branches, improving traceability and team coordination.

Commenting features, available with a Cameo Collaborator license, provide flexible ways to collaborate on specific elements and to view all comments of the project with easy navigation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Integration and Data Synchronization

CATIA DataHub now supports the **Copy Data** operation for **SysML v2 projects**, available for **DOORS**, **DOORS Next (DNG)**, **JAMA Connect**, and **ENOVIA Requirements**connectors, with standard properties such as **Declared Name**and **Documentation Body**included in mapping support.

400249

ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zX0UR_ibLbw" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3><strong>Model <span>Simulation</span></strong></h3><p>Bridge the gap between static design and dynamic validation with the <strong>SysML v2 Simulation</strong> that transforms structural architectures into <strong>executable, verifiable system representations</strong> early in the lifecycle.</p><p>CATIA Magic/Cameo delivers a robust execution environment designed to make analysis seamless:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5d06657e-e84c-4a22-99ab-e46ea3c977f8"><strong>State Machine Execution - </strong>execute complex state machine logic alongside structural connectivity to verify <strong>state-based behaviors</strong> and <strong>system interactions</strong> before prototyping.</li><li data-uuid="b823e1ba-aab6-44da-b55d-169de9ce6efa"><strong>Live Visual Verification</strong> - track execution paths in <strong>real time</strong> with intuitive model <strong>highlights</strong>, dynamic <strong>runtime</strong> <strong>value</strong> updates, and <strong>automatic requirements verification</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="2420d886-9051-425d-bc38-58245e42fd0d"><strong>Interactive Debugging</strong> - change initial values mid-execution, manually fire transitions, or inject triggers to rapidly analyze edge cases and unexpected operational scenarios</li><li data-uuid="559c9808-ecad-4dc7-9f53-2ad695bba295"><strong>Time-Series Plotting &amp; Data Export</strong> – monitor complex numerical variables over time using the <strong>Time Series Chart</strong>, or <strong>export</strong> complete execution datasets to <strong>CSV</strong> for downstream verification and analysis.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="670"><ri:attachment ri:filename="runtime_values_in_views.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="670"><ri:attachment ri:filename="water_tank_simulation.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><strong style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Model Validation</strong></h3><p>Maintaining model quality is essential for ensuring consistency, correctness, and compliance with modeling standards. CATIA Magic/Cameo introduces comprehensive model validation capabilities that automatically evaluate models against validation rules provided by SysML, KerML, and Dassault Systèmes predefined validation suites.</p><p>CATIA Magic/Cameo provides multiple ways to validate and review model content:</p><ul><li data-uuid="965a1018-d880-4a12-9cad-4276e035dae7">Validation in views – failing elements are highlighted directly in views, enabling immediate identification of issues. Detailed validation results can be accessed through element smart manipulators, while the Validation Results panel provides a consolidated overview of all failures within the current view.</li><li data-uuid="11391f61-2883-4e9e-86f1-f6a9f522a392">Validation in the Textual Editor – textual notation is validated automatically as you work. Failing elements are underlined, validation details are available on hover, allowing rapid issue identification without leaving the editor.</li><li data-uuid="33f0bee1-63c7-409c-a4e5-ca5a396788f4">Specific scope validation – validate specific scope together with all recursively contained elements using the Validate command, available from both the Containment tree.</li><li data-uuid="b09a1453-1cc0-43b8-82a7-92f9ea839279">Custom validation rules – create model-based validation suites and rules to enforce organization specific modeling standards.</li><li data-uuid="e898e4e0-a722-46e1-9df4-93c6a7572d43">Validation suite management – enable or disable predefined and custom validation suites through project settings, providing flexibility to align validation behavior with project requirements.</li></ul><p>By integrating validation directly into graphical, tabular, and textual modeling workflows, CATIA Magic/Cameo helps teams detect issues earlier, maintain higher model quality, and ensure adherence to modeling standards throughout the development lifecycle.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="670"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-6-22_15-49-7.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="670"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-6-22_15-49-26.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b802982-c47a-4fb7-8406-3d498d5a78d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0BCAk6hI4SY" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Adopt SysML v2 at Your Own Pace</h3><p>Organizations can adopt SysML v2 at their own pace thanks to<span> the </span><strong>full co-existence of SysML v2 with SysML v1</strong>. Both versions can be authored in the same CATIA Magic/Cameo modeling environment, with the ability to select SysML v1 or v2 per project. Migration is supported by the SysML v2 transformation specification, ensuring legacy models remain usable and protecting existing investments.</p><p>Key features:</p><ul><li data-uuid="42956569-8409-4b10-88e1-126f5d52cff8"><strong>Authoring</strong><span> </span>– co-existence of SysML v1 and v2 in the same environment<ul><li>Choose v1 or v2 on a per-project basis</li><li>Support for SysML v2 transformation specifications for smooth migration, including diagrams</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="5d3ceee0-9ef1-4b39-a722-9dd765c969dc"><strong>API</strong><span> </span>– server REST API continuity<ul><li>Full SysML v2 standard API support</li><li>Continue using existing server REST API for both v1 and v2 projects</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b4d728dc-8e9d-44df-85ac-143401ebab19"><strong>Simulation</strong><span> </span>– use case continuity from v1<ul><li>Core SysML v1 simulation use cases supported in SysML v2</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="284aaa31-addf-4e2b-bc65-f30f4ac0df73"><strong>User experience</strong><span> </span>– focus on usability<ul><li>Best features from SysML v1 implementation reused to maximize user efficiency</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Progress.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a938004f-9ff3-4ab2-b4cc-89b5ddbd6e52"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P7B2nzaT-F4" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-icon-accent-blue,#1d7afc);"><strong> </strong></span></p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Standard SysML v2 REST API: CI/CD workflows, automation, querying, model evaluation</span></h3><p>Teamwork Cloud fully conforms to the<span> </span><strong>SysML v2 API and Services</strong><span> </span>specification, ensuring interoperability with external tools and deployment of automated MBSE workflows.</p><ul><li data-uuid="148fcfbc-3b46-4dab-a77c-1f6f7b1e1e3e"><strong>Programmatic access</strong><span> </span>– create, edit, query, retrieve, and evaluate models directly from scripts or applications.</li><li data-uuid="f82e2378-8332-4bab-b91d-3e6251b16a7a"><strong>Seamless integration</strong><span> </span>– connect with data analytics platforms, DevOps CI/CD pipelines, and custom engineering workflows.</li><li data-uuid="0bbe791a-e081-4194-acc1-3bc3f31d5145"><strong>Custom API extensions</strong><span> </span>– query and analyze SysML v2 model more efficiently with CATIA Magic's home-grown Model Evaluation services.<span> </span>Stay tuned for more exciting API extensions!</li></ul><p>This combination enables engineers to effectively<span> </span><strong>navigate, query, and analyze SysML v2 models</strong><span> </span>with full standard compliance and interoperability in mind.</p><h3>Collaborative Modeling Through Teamwork Cloud</h3><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud (TWC)</strong> enables secure, lock-free collaborative modeling for SysML v2.</p><ul><li data-uuid="33952c15-040b-489a-b5a0-ccdbea62444a"><strong>Simultaneous collaboration</strong> – multiple users can work on the same model at the same time.</li><li data-uuid="30fd8ae1-73e4-48dd-b01d-efa46b511933"><strong>Automatic merging</strong> – non-conflicting changes are merged automatically; intuitive resolution for overlaps.</li><li data-uuid="d85ac6a5-6a77-4460-915d-09d6d70356c9"><strong>Versioned &amp; modular models</strong> – decomposition into manageable units, supporting efficient collaboration across teams, organizations, and geographies. Supports collaborative development in branches for experimentation, with reintegration through a model patch mechanism.</li><li data-uuid="85a41ae8-9c73-4def-947f-39a8438c4e0b"><strong>Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)</strong> – fine-grained permissions at the project or branch level to protect data access, ensuring that users and user groups have appropriate access rights.</li><li data-uuid="a7f85ed0-252c-4c59-9e9d-89e80a1d553b"><strong>Support for U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) requirements</strong> <ul><li>Provides support for Data Markings/Classification based on the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification, allowing to mark security-sensitive data such as users and projects with custom-defined data markings</li><li>Data spillage handling via dedicated tools for physical removal of model data affected by data spillage incidents</li><li>On-premise deployment for isolated classified programs</li><li>Prime-sub model exchange workflows built with scalability and IP protection in mind</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="47a33cee-c1f3-4011-bc99-1f3d5c303b37"><strong>Standards-based SAML authentication integrations</strong> – supports single sign-on including built-in, LDAP, PKI/CAC, and SAML-based authentication.</li><li data-uuid="d00676dd-3213-44d7-be6e-04315d6eaee4"><strong>Auditing</strong> – provides access to audit reports to track user access and permissions granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.</li><li data-uuid="73e89a5a-be77-421a-8d62-99afa9fa5d6c"><strong>Cross-cluster synchronization</strong> – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.</li></ul><p>Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).</p><p style="text-align: center;"><span class="cke-highlighted"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0d901f2-717a-4087-9d4f-b3145b791ffe"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x1lLHDEvusk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p><h3>MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)</h3><p>MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.<span style="text-align: center;letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 2.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 3.png" /></ac:image></p><p><strong>MagicLab Collaborator</strong></p><p><span>Introducing MagicLab Collaborator, a service that enables teams to collaborate on SysML v2 projects. Building on the capabilities of MagicLab, which is a read-only viewer, MagicLab Collaborator includes flexible collaboration features such as branch-specific feedback, commenting with search and editing, and support for working across multiple branches. Users can also open and review both current and historical branches, improving traceability and team coordination.</span></p><p><span>Commenting features, available with a Cameo Collaborator license, provide flexible ways to collaborate on specific elements and to view all comments of the project with easy navigation.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="670"><ri:attachment ri:filename="magiclab_individual_element_comments.png" /></ac:image></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Integration and Data Synchronization</h3><p>CATIA DataHub now supports the <strong>Copy Data</strong> operation for <strong>SysML v2 projects</strong>, available for <strong>DOORS</strong>, <strong>DOORS Next (DNG)</strong>, <strong>JAMA Connect</strong>, and <strong>ENOVIA Requirements </strong>connectors, with standard properties such as <strong>Declared Name </strong>and <strong>Documentation Body </strong>included in mapping support.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82623487-4895-4dc2-9919-9612c8df4b09"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/tfuscq0Gzb4?si=pbLHT39sm1cYCLmU" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006455 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2026x Refresh1) Command Palette

- page_id: `304006455`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006455/2026x+Refresh1+Command+Palette
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:30.156+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to using commands listed in the Textual Editor shortcut menu, you can use the Command Palette, which contains a multitude of additional commands.You can execute a command by clicking on it in the palette or by using shortcut keys if any are assigned.

##### Invoking the Command Palette

To invoke the Command Palette

Do one of the following:

- Right-click anywhere in the Textual Editor. In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.
- Press F1 on the keyboard.

##### Quick-access symbols table

The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the first symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.

| Symbol | Name | Description and Usage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| : | Go to Line/Column | This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.Shortcut keys: Ctrl+G |
| @ | Go to Symbol | This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. Shortcut keys: Ctrl+Shift+OUse up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation. |
| @: | Go to Symbol by Category | This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.) |
| > | Show and Run Commands | This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned. To search for a specific command type its name after the > symbol, e.g., '>fold'.This is the default quick-access symbol that is displayed in the search field once the Command Palette is displayed. Useful commands'Toggle High Contrast Theme' - switches the editor to dark mode. |
| (empty) | - | If the search field is empty, select the relevant symbol from the displayed menu containing all quick-access symbols. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.</li><li>Press F1 on the keyboard.</li></ul><h3>Quick-access symbols table </h3><p>The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the <u>first</u> symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Symbol</th><th>Name</th><th>Description and Usage</th></tr><tr><td>:</td><td>Go to Line/Column</td><td><p>This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.</p><p><strong>Shortcut keys:</strong> Ctrl+G</p></td></tr><tr><td>@</td><td>Go to Symbol</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. </p><p><strong>Shortcut keys: </strong>Ctrl+Shift+O</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68742d22-1498-4fca-b08a-606f64a9f7a0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>@:</td><td>Go to Symbol by Category</td><td><p>This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.)</p></td></tr><tr><td>&gt;</td><td>Show and Run Commands</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned. To search for a specific command type its name after the &gt; symbol, e.g., '&gt;fold'.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d05c3477-1a8e-4279-a5de-035da62fffd8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is the default quick-access symbol that is displayed in the search field once the Command Palette is displayed. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="635fa275-68e9-4fa1-b123-8cbbbe770137"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>'Toggle High Contrast Theme' - switches the editor to dark mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>(empty)</td><td>-</td><td><p>If the search field is empty, select the relevant symbol from the displayed menu containing all quick-access symbols.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005386 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00030: (2026x Refresh1) Compartments

- page_id: `304005386`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005386/2026x+Refresh1+Compartments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:29.603+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with views > (2026x Refresh1) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A compartment view usage is an area on the element symbol that displays specific element data. You can also use compartments to. Any usage or definition element may have compartments listing theirownedand inherited elements. The element symbol compartments are a graphical display of the element body in the textual notation.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

requirementdefMassLimitationRequirement{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be less than or equal to the required mass. */ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassReqd:MassValue; 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=massReqd 
} 
}

###### On the left: the requirement symbol with *documentation*, *attributes*, and *require constraints* compartments. 
On the right: the same requirement with its owned elements declared in the body.

##### Displaying and suppressing compartments

Many symbols have various compartments available to them which are hidden by default. Follow the the options listed below to check it if the needed compartment is not displayed.

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- In the view, click the symbol, then click the **Compartments**button. Select or deselect the particular compartment. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the view, right-click the symbol and in its shortcut menu, click Symbol Style . Then enable or disable the Show Compartment property for the needed compartment in the **Symbol Style**dialog .
- In the view, select a symbol and click the Suppress <compartment name> button. [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_compartment.png']

##### Displaying or hiding elements in compartments

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in symbol compartments.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog

- Select a symbol and click the Compartments button. In the submenu, click the arrow to expand and then click Edit Compartments . [ATTACHMENT filename='compartment_edit_dialog.png']

##### Creating elements via compartments

You can use compartments to create new owned elements for the selected element.

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_the_element_symbol_in_a_diagram.png']
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the compartment of the element kind you want to create, click the Create <element kind> button. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_compartment_in_a_diagram.png']

##### Compartments table

The table below lists the compartments available to specific element symbols based on their element metaclass.

| Compartment | Element metaclass |  | Compartment | Element metaclass |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| metadata | All namespaces |  | perfom actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionStateDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageStateUsage |
| doc | PackageDefinitionsUsages, excluding:IfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | subject | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| actors | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | stakeholders | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| objective | CaseUsageCaseDefinition |  | include use cases | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageExhibitStateUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |
| parameters | ConstraintUsageConstraintDefinitionActionUsageActionDefinition |  | frames | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| satisfy requirements | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |  | exhibit states | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| require constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | attributes | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| assume constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | items | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages |
| assert constraints | PartUsagePartDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | enums | EnumerationDefinition |
| constraints | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| parts | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | ends | ConnectorAsUsageConnectionDefinition |
| ports | PartUsagePartDefinitionPortUsagePortDefinition |  | members | Package |
| while condition | WhileActionUsage |  | features (name not displayed in compartment) | MetadataUsage |
| until condition | WhileActionUsage |  | rules | Part usage or definition specializing Policies::StyleSheet |
| for iterator | ForLoopActionUsage |  |  |  |
| if condition | IfActionUsageElseAction |  |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A compartment view usage is an area on the element symbol that displays specific element data. You can also use compartments to </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create new owned elements for the selected element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Any usage or definition element may have compartments listing their </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e1e3fb5f-79a4-486d-b2aa-ff8789bdc300">owned</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and inherited elements. The element symbol compartments are a graphical display of the element body in the textual notation.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67ca0926-f2fb-42fe-b1f2-ed136224fc90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirement_symbol_with_compartments.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;">On the left: the requirement symbol with <em>documentation</em>, <em>attributes</em>, and <em>require constraints</em> compartments.<br /><span>On the right: the same requirement with its owned elements declared in the body.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Displaying and suppressing compartments</h3><p>Many symbols have various compartments available to them which are hidden by default. Follow the the options listed below <span>to check it if the needed compartment is not displayed.</span></p><p>To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>In the view, click the symbol, then click the <strong>Compartments </strong>button. Select or deselect the particular compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments_button.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the view, right-click the symbol and in its shortcut menu, click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Symbol Style</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Then enable or disable the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Show Compartment</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> property for the needed compartment </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">in the <strong>Symbol Style </strong>dialog</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>In the view, select a symbol and click the <strong>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</strong> button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="suppress_compartment.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Displaying or hiding elements in compartments </h3><p>Use the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in symbol compartments.</p><p>To open the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a symbol and click the <strong>Compartments </strong>button. In the submenu, click the arrow to expand and then click <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_edit_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3><span>Creating elements via compartments </span></h3><p>You can use compartments to create new owned elements for the selected element.</p><p><span>To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select an element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_the_element_symbol_in_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select an element symbol and on the right side of the compartment of the element kind you want to create, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>&lt;element kind&gt; </span></strong>button. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_compartment_in_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Compartments table</h3><p>The table below lists the compartments available to specific element symbols based on their element metaclass.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 34.6916%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 21.4008%;" /><col class="" style="width: 24.4581%;" /><col class="" style="width: 7.49029%;" /><col class="" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004684 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00031: (2026x Refresh1) Concerns

- page_id: `304004684`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004684/2026x+Refresh1+Concerns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:42.175+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A concern is used to specify the concerns of one or more stakeholders.One or more concerns can then be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Frame concern' space=''] in other requirement definitions and usages.

##### Creating concern usage and definition elements

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *concern def*for a concern definition.
  2. *concern* for a concern usage.
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the element in the element body. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

concerndefClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient:Clients; 
doc/* Client concerns regarding the vehicle mass, functionality, and design. */ 
} 
concernmassConcern:ClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient1:Clients; 
requireconstraint{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be equal to the required mass. */ 
} 
}

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. concern def to create a concern definition element.
  2. concern to create a concern usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **concern** def button to create a concern definition element.
  2. The **concern** button to create a concern usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **concern**or the **concern****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the concern symbol.
3. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* The actual mass shall be equal to the required mass. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>concern def</strong> to create a concern definition element.</li><li><strong>concern </strong>to create a concern usage element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the concern.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdb0cced-2467-41dd-81b9-f357161ce9bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the procedures on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating concern usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create concern usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>concern</strong></span> <strong>def </strong>button to create a concern definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>concern</strong></span> button to create a concern usage element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <strong>concern </strong>or the <strong>concern</strong><strong> def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the concern symbol.</li><li>Specify the concern.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa39b043-aa22-4254-86c5-56510b86adac"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006087 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: (2026x Refresh1) Connection

- page_id: `304006087`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006087/2026x+Refresh1+Connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:00.805+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A connection definition is a relationship that classifies a connection between related things, such as items and parts. A connection usage relates features to connector ends from the connection definition via a reference subsetting relationship, associating those ends with the specific usage elements that are to be connected. Connections can be either binary, with two connection ends, or n-ary, with multiple ends. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) N-ary connection' space=''] page.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross features' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *pressureSeatConn* connection connector where *bead* reference subsets *t.bead* connecting to *mountingRim*which reference subsets *w.rim*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating connection usage and definition elements

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
} 
partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partt:Tire[1] { 
partbead:TireBead[2]; 
} 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
}// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectt.beadtow.rim;// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(t.bead,w.rim); 
// unnamed unspecialized connection usage with named ends connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(beadEnd ::>t.bead,rimEnd ::>w.rim);// unnamed specialized (feature typing) connection usage connecting featureswith explicit declaration: connection:PressureSeatconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConnconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting featureswith explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConn: PressureSeat connect bead ::> t.bead to mountingRim ::> w.rim; 
// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list:connectionpressureSeatConn:PressureSeatconnect(bead::>t.bead,mountingRim::>w.rim);} 
}

###### Creating connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the connection command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Create Relation command

To create connection usage elements via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click connection . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create a connection definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** def button to create a connection definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.
- To create a connection usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** button to create a connection usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection, n-ary connection**or the **connection****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword connection def .
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
}

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command

To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click connection def for connection definition.

###### Auto-creating connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:</span></span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span></span>connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pressureSeatConn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">t</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;                                      </span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span typing) connection usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with named ends </span></span>connecting <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">features </span></span>with explicit declaration:</span></span><br />        </span>connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pressureSeatConn </span>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">t</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingRim</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim</span>;   <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span></span>// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with named ends </span></span>connecting features in a comma-separated list:</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span>connection<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pressureSeatConn </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>connect<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">t</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead,</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>connection </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage elements <span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>connection</strong>. The <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create a connection definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create a connection definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create a connection usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><span>button to create a connection usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>connection, n-ary connection </strong></span>or the <span><strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span> </span><em>connection def</em><span>.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the needed element. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireBead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>connection def</strong> for connection definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005727 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00034: (2026x Refresh1) Constraints

- page_id: `304005727`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005727/2026x+Refresh1+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:44.592+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A constraint definition defines a logical condition, and a constraint usage applies this condition in different contexts, enabling the reuse of the constraint definition throughout the system. The constraint usage is satisfied when its expression evaluates to true, and violated when it does not.

##### Creating constraint usage and definition elements

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. constraint def for a constraint definition.
  2. *constraint* for a constraint usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintdefMassLimit{ // constraint definition 
attributemass:MassValue; 
attributemassLimit:MassValue; 
mass<=massLimit 
}

partvehicle{ 
attribute massActual : MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

constraintmassConstraint { massActual<=massRequired }// named constraint usage 
constraint{ massActual<=massRequired }// unnamed constraint usage 
}

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. constraint def to create a constraint definition element.
  2. constraint to create a constraint usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **constraint** def button to create a constraint definition element.
  2. The **constraint** button to create a constraint usage element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the constraint.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Auto-creating constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.
