data-uuid="f0a10f1a-6f3f-4d22-9f32-6208a3524b5c"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="2d59ca82-c23e-4e3d-91d9-50c5ccd52164">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>constraint def</strong> to create a constraint definition element.</li><li><strong>constraint </strong>to create a constraint usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e4478f14-da16-449a-a293-07e7a04c1824">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="cf013236-e684-4ece-ac19-16abcf79af6d">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).</li></ol><h4>Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="133ee16b-1d89-44a6-8078-328e7588ad13">In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>constraint </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create a constraint definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>constraint </strong></span>button to create a constraint usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f9e22379-9821-4501-8ac1-015956b9d3b8">Click in the view pane where you want to create the constraint.</li><li data-uuid="ea7127a1-bf8d-4ec9-be1b-76b8f6f66a26">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="ee8691fd-1c8d-48d5-9a61-75f587b82a9f"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006098 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00035: (2026x Refresh1) Control Functions

- page_id: `304006098`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006098/2026x+Refresh1+Control+Functions
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:40:13.179+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists the functions available in the*ControlFunctions*namespace of the Standard Libraries.

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

| Function | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| dotabstract function . { in ref feature source[0..*] : Anything {chain}} | package ChainTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; part person { attribute age = 20; } attribute personAge = person.age;}Output>> personAge20 | Accesses a property or feature of a model element or structure. |
| ifabstract function if { in ref feature test[1] : Boolean in thenValue[0..1] in elseValue[0..1]} | package IfTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; attribute userAge = 17; attribute ifTest = if userAge > 18 ? "adult" else "child";}Output>> ifTestchild | Evaluates a Boolean condition and returns thenValue if condition is true; otherwise, returns elseValue. |
| andfunction and { in ref feature firstValue[1] : Boolean in secondValue[0..1]} | package AndTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; attribute travelerAge = 20; attribute hasValidPassport = true; attribute flightEligibility = if travelerAge >= 18 & hasValidPassport ? "clearedToFlyAbroad" else "notClearedToFlyAbroad"; }Output>> flightEligibilityclearedToFlyAbroad | Returns true if both Boolean expressions are true. |
| orfunction or { in ref feature firstValue[1] : Boolean in secondValue[0..1]} | package OrTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; attribute travelerAge = 16; attribute hasParentalPermission = true; attribute flightEligibility = if (travelerAge > 17 \| hasParentalPermission) ? "clearedToFlyAbroad" else "notClearedToFlyAbroad"; }Output>> flightEligibilityclearedToFlyAbroad | Returns true if either x or y, or both, evaluate to true. |
| collectabstract function collect { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Anything in mapper[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : Anything }} | package CollectTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; package modelFragment { part engine; part gearbox; action assemble; } attribute nestedPartsInFragment = modelFragment.metadata->collect { in pkg : Package; pkg.member as PartUsage }; }Output>> nestedPartsInFragmentMetadataFeature PartUsage engine MetadataFeature PartUsage gearbox | Applies an expression to each element in a collection and returns a new collection of results (map operation). |
| selectabstract function select { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Anything in selector[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : Anything }} | package SelectTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; package modelFragment1 { part engine; action assemble; } package modelFragment2 { part gearbox; } attribute onlyPartsInPackage = (modelFragment1.metadata, modelFragment2.metadata)->select { in pkg : Package; pkg.member istype PartUsage }; }Output>> onlyPartsInPackageMetadataFeature Package modelFragment2 | Returns a collection of all elements that satisfy a Boolean condition (filter). |
| selectOnefunction selectOne { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Anything in selector1[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : Anything }} | package SelectOneTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; package modelFragment { part engine; part gearbox; } attribute onlyPartsInPackage = (modelFragment meta Package).ownedMember->selectOne { in member; member istype PartUsage }; }Output>> onlyPartsInPackageMetadataFeature PartUsage engine | Returns the first element in the collection x for which the selector1 condition evaluates to true. |
| rejectabstract function reject { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Anything in rejector[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : Anything }} | package RejectTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; package modelFragment { part engine; action accelerate; } attribute removeParts = (modelFragment meta Package).ownedMember->reject { in member; member istype PartUsage }; }Output>> removePartsMetadataFeature ActionUsage accelerate | Returns a subcollection of collection x excluding elements for which the rejector condition evaluates to true. |
| forAllabstract function forAll { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Anything in test[0..*]} | package forAllTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; part car { attribute tirePressureFront = 5; attribute tirePressureRear = 5; } attribute allTirePressuresAreNominal = (car.tirePressureFront, car.tirePressureRear)->forAll { in pressure; pressure == 5 }; }Output>> allTirePressuresAreNominaltrue | Returns true if the test condition holds for all elements in collection x. |
| allTruefunction allTrue { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Boolean} | package AllTrueTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; part car { attribute hasSpareTire = true; attribute hasFirstAidKit = true; } attribute hasRequiredSafetyItems = allTrue(car.hasSpareTire, car.hasFirstAidKit); }Output>> hasRequiredSafetyItemstrue | Returns true if all Boolean elements in collection x evaluate to true. |
| anyTruefunction anyTrue { in ref feature collection[0..*] : Boolean} | package AnyTrueTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; part car { attribute hasSpareTire = false; attribute hasFirstAidKit = false; } attribute hasAnySafetyItem = anyTrue(car.hasSpareTire, car.hasFirstAidKit);}Output>> hasAnySafetyItemfalse | Returns true if at least one Boolean element in collection x evaluates to true. |
| minimizefunction minimize { in ref feature collection[1..*] : ScalarValue in fn[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : ScalarValue }} | package MinimizeTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; part drone { attribute leg1LengthCm = 32; attribute leg2LengthCm = 28; attribute leg3LengthCm = 35; } attribute shortestLandingLegCm = (drone.leg1LengthCm, drone.leg2LengthCm, drone.leg3LengthCm)->minimize { in x; x }; }Output>> shortestLandingLegCm28 | Returns the element of collection x for which the condition fn has the smallest value. |
| maximizefunction maximize { in ref feature collection[1..*] : ScalarValue in fn[0..*] { in ref feature argument[1] : ScalarValue }} | package MaximizeTest { private import ControlFunctions::*; private import SysML::*; part drone { attribute leg1LengthCm = 32; attribute leg2LengthCm = 28; attribute leg3LengthCm = 35; } attribute longestLandingLegCm = (drone.leg1LengthCm, drone.leg2LengthCm, drone.leg3LengthCm)->maximize { in x; x };}Output>> longestLandingLegCm35 | Returns the element of collection x for which the condition fn has the largest value. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005081 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00036: (2026x Refresh1) Control nodes

- page_id: `304005081`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005081/2026x+Refresh1+Control+nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:05.346+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions > (2026x Refresh1) Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The sequencing of action usages is controlled using control nodes. Control nodes are always connected to other action usages by incoming and outgoing successions (with or without guards). Kinds of control nodes include the following:

- Fork node
  - Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.
  - The actions connected to the outgoing successions cannot start until the action connected to the incoming succession has completed.
- Join node
  - Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession.
  - The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until all the actions connected to the incoming successions have completed.
- Decision node
  - Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.
  - Once the action connected to the incoming succession has completed, exactly one of the actions connected to an outgoing succession can start based on the guard condition of the outgoing succession that is satisfied (the else condition is the complement of all other guard conditions). See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Guard conditions' space=''] page.
- Merge node
  - Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession.
  - The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until any one of the actions connected to an incoming succession has completed.

##### Creating control nodes

###### Creating control nodes via the Textual Editor

To create control nodes via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *fork*for a fork node.
  2. join for a join node.
  3. decide for a decision node
  4. merge for a merge node.
2. (Optional) Specify the node name .
3. Click the Synchronize button. actiontransportPassenger{ 
firststart; 
thenactionenterVehicle; 
thenactionstartVehicle; 
thenmergeNode; 
mergemergeNode;// named merge node declaration; it waits for the 'startVehicle'action to happen before continuingthenforkNode; 
forkforkNode;// namedfork node declaration; it enables all its successors to happen after it 
thenactiondriveVehicle; // the source of this succession is the fork node 
thenactionprovidePower;// the source of this succession is the fork node 
thenjoinNode; 
thenjoinNode; 
joinjoinNode;// namedjoin node declaration; it waits for all its predecessors to happen before continuing 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
thendecideNode; 
decidedecideNode; // nameddecide node declaration; typically followed by one or more conditional successions from which it chooses exactly one to happen after it 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // references the merge node named 'mergeNode' as the target of the succession 
elsedone; 
}

###### Creating control nodes via the view palette

To create control nodes via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. **fork**for a fork node.
  2. join for a join node.
  3. decide for a decision node
  4. merge for a merge node.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the node symbol.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">providePower</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   // the source of this succession is the fork node</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">       <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">joinNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">       <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">joinNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">join</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">joinNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                       </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">named </span>join node declaration; it waits for all its predecessors to happen before continuing</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">       <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">decide</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">             </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">named </span>decide node declaration; typically followed by one or more conditional successions from which it chooses exactly one to happen after it </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">       <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// references the merge node named '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode</span>' as the target of the succession</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">       <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">else</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">done</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating control nodes via the view palette</h4><p>To create <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">control nodes via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>fork </strong>for a fork node.</p></li><li><strong>join </strong>for a join node.</li><li><strong>decide </strong>for a decision node</li><li><strong>merge </strong>for a merge node.</li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the node symbol.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004868 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00037: (2026x Refresh1) Controlling actions

- page_id: `304004868`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004868/2026x+Refresh1+Controlling+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:49.934+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can control actions using the following elements:

- **Start**and **done**actions that allow you to specify the start and end of an action sequence.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Start and done actions' space=''] page.
- Control nodes , such as decision, merge, fork, and join nodes that allow you to control the sequencing of action usages.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Control nodes' space=''] page.
- **Guard conditions**that allow you to specify conditional succession.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Guard conditions' space=''] page.
- Structured control actions , such as if action usage, while action usage, for action usage ****that allow you to control the performance of nested action usages in a structured way.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Structured control actions' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can control actions using the following elements:</span></p><ul><li><span><strong>Start </strong>and <strong>done </strong>actions that <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">allow you to specify the start and end of an action sequence.</span></span><ul><li><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Start and done actions" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></li></ul></li><li><strong>Control nodes</strong>, such as decision, merge, fork, and join nodes that allow you to control the sequencing of action usages.<ul><li>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Control nodes" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Guard conditions </strong>that allow you to specify conditional succession.</span><ul><li><span>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Guard conditions" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></li><li><strong>Structured control actions</strong>, such as if action usage, while action usage, for action usage<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span><strong> </strong>that allow you to <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">control the performance of nested action usages in a structured way.</span></span></span><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Structured control actions" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></span></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005087 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00038: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a style sheet condition

- page_id: `304005087`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005087/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+style+sheet+condition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:28:32.407+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify a condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the condition specified for the entire style sheet.

- If you specify a condition for the entire style sheet, ensure the style sheet is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] , not a single view, for the condition to take effect.
- Currently, the style sheet condition can only be created via the Textual Editor.

###### Creating a style sheet condition

To create a condition for an entire style sheet

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] in the Textual Editor.
2. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, redefine the *styleSheetCondition*element inherited from the StyleSheet element and define it by the needed condition.
3. Within the body of the condition, declare a ref item , then r edefine the needed item and specify its values. The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
4. You can now:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] .
    1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] .
    2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] .
  2. Synchronize modifications and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet{ 
 part:>>styleSheetCondition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByDiagramTypeCondition{// style sheet condition targeting symbols by view definition 
refitem:>>diagramType=DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata;// the condition targets views defined by the *General View* view definition 
} 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006245 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00039: (2026x Refresh1) Creating actions

- page_id: `304006245`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006245/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:15.010+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An action definition is a kind of occurrence definition that defines action performances. An action usage is a kind of occurrence usage that is a usage of one or more action definitions performed in a specific context. Actions coordinate the performance of other actions and generate effects on items and parts involved in the performance.

##### Creating action usage and definition elements

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *action def*for an action definition.
  2. action for an action usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

actiondefTakePicture;// action definition

actiontakePicture:TakePicture;// action usage

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. action def to create an action definition element.
  2. action to create an action usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create action usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **action** def button to create an action definition element.
  2. The **action** button to create an action usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **action**or the **action def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the action symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an action symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the action element.
  - Select an action symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create action button. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Creating action usage elements via the Specification panel

To create action usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **action**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **actions**compartment section's bar, click the **Create action**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

###### Auto-creating action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

To auto-create action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create action usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>action </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create an action definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>action </strong></span>button to create an action usage element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <strong>action </strong>or the <strong>action def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the action symbol.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the action's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view </h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /></ac:link> page.</p><p>To create action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view </p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select an action symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>action </strong>element. </li><li>Select an action symbol and on the right side of the <strong>actions </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>action </span></strong>button. <br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>actions </strong>compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating action usage elements via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create action usage elements via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>action </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>actions </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create action</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>actions</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Auto-c<span>reating action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands</span></h4><p><span>To auto-create action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005120 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00040: (2026x Refresh1) Creating custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `304005120`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005120/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:09.746+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views > (2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom symbolic view definition creation with custom and/or inherited view palettes, and predefined and/or custom templated buttons in custom and/or inherited button categories.

The procedures on this page use the example of creating custom symbolic view definitions by customizing the*DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv***standard predefined symbolic view definition. This is the recommended workflow for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''], as the *Base Symbolic View* definition carries the predefined view palette, which contains Selection, Tools, and Common button categories, as well as the rendering feature, providing a flexible basis for creating custom symbolic view definitions.However, you can similarly customize any other symbolic view definition, whether [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views' space=''] or custom.

#### NOTE: Updating custom view definition

Updating custom view definition

Reload the project for changes made to the custom symbolic view definition to take effect.

##### Creating custom symbolic view definitions

To create custom symbolic view definitions

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette* package.
5. Declare a view definition, specify the name for the new symbolic view definition, subclassify the Base Symbolic View definition from the DS_Views::CoreViews package, then within the body of the view definition.
6. Click the Synchronize button. The new view definition becomes available in theonce you:[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating View Creation dialog' space=''].Add the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating View Creation dialog' space='']. This requires you to .

package'Custom symbolic view definitions' { // package for the custom symbolic view definition(s)privateimportDS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::*; 
viewdef'Requirements View':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View' subclassifying'bsv', i.e., *'Base Symbolic View'*definition 
// specify the view palette here}}

##### Creating palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

To create palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

1. Within the body of the view definition element of the new symbolic view definition, redefine the baseViewPalette inherited from the Base Symbolic View definition.
2. Specify button categories by doing any of the following:
  1. 
  2. 
3. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View'part:>>baseViewPalette{ // redefines the *baseViewPalette* inherited from the *Base Symbolic View* definition. The custom palette now inherits Selection, Tools, and Common button categories// specify button categorieshere} 
}

##### Managing button categories in custom symbolic view palettes

The procedures in this subsection explain how to manage inherited and custom button categories and their attributes.

###### Removing inherited button categories

To remove inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button category you want to remove. The button categories that are inherited from the *baseViewPalette* are *selectionCategory*, *toolsCategory*, and *commonCategory*. You can find them in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find the button categories in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
3. Set the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>> toolsCategory[0];// unnamed part redefines *toolsCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette,*specifying the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero to remove it} 
}

###### Adding custom button categories

To add custom button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, and then subset *buttonCategories*.
3. Within the new button category's body, do any of the following:
  1. .
  2. .
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories; // new button category instance} 
}

###### Specifying attributes for custom or inherited button categories

To specify attributes for custom or inherited button categories

1. Within the body of the button category element, redefine the attributes that you want to modify with the needed values. All button categories - inherited and custom ones - inherit all the same button category attributes from *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::AbstractItem* and *ItemsCategory*, which are: *isCollapsed*, *hasVisibleLabels*, *label*, *description.*You can also find the attributes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ 
part:>>baseViewPalette{ 
// modifying an inherited button category's attributes: 
part:>>selectionCategory{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Selection";// modified category name 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified category default collapse state 
} 
// specifying the custom button category's attributes: 
partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories{ 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse;// newly specified *isCollapsed* attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue;// newly specified *hasVisibleLabels*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Requirements";// newly specified *label*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>descriptiondefault"Requirements";// newly specified *description*attribute for the custom button category 
}} 
}

##### Managing buttons in button categories

A button category can contain specific and/or menu buttons that contain specific buttons in its submenu. You can also specify one of the submenu buttons as the focused button that would be displayed as the default one for the menu button. Additionally, you can create templates for custom buttons, allowing you to add custom templated buttons to custom and/or inherited button categories.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

###### Creating menu buttons in button categories

To create menu buttons in button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the button category element*.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *ButtonsCategory,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Add submenu buttons for the menu button:
  1. .
  2. .
4. (Optional) Specify the focused button:
  1. Redefine *focusedButton*, and specify the focused button by indicating the name of the needed button's part element.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;}} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; // the part *reqButton* is specified as the focused (default) button for the menu button 
}}} 
}

###### Adding predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in*direction with the code action identifier based on the type of element you want the button to create. You can find the needed code action identifier for the element kind you want the button to create in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers*package. It looks like this:attributerequirementAction : ScalarValues::String="ADD_REQUIREMENT"; // the code action identifier for the requirement usage element button is *requirementAction*
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;} // specifies *requirementAction* so that this button creates the requirement usage element} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} // specifies *requirementDefAction* so that this button creates the requirement definition element 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; 
}}} 
}

###### Removing buttons from inherited button categories

To remove buttons from inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button you want to remove. See the buttons specified for each predefined button category in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
3. Set the redefining button's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>>selectionCategory{ // unnamed part redefines *selectionCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette*

// ------ predefined button:partcompleteCoverageModeButton :DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::Button:> abstractButtons

// ------ modified button (instead of the notation above):part:>>completeCoverageModeButton[0]; // multiplicity for the button *completeCoverageModeButton*is set to zero to remove it}} 
}

##### Managing custom templated buttons in button categories

Instead of or in addition to using predefined buttons either by adding them to your custom or inherited button categories, you can also create your own custom buttons. For that, first, you need to create a template for the custom button, and then you can add it to a specific button category of the view palette.

###### Creating templates for custom buttons

The following procedure depicts how to create an element template using metadata to extend the requirement usage element with the needed attribute, where the requirement usage element is named *Software*. Then the template can be passed into the custom button specification using *metadataAccessExpression*.

To create templates for custom buttons

1. Within the package containing the new symbolic view definition, d eclare:
  1. A metadata definition element with the needed name and attributes.
  2. A package element for the template.
    1. Within the template element, apply metadata to the element kind you want to extend and specify the element name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

package'Custom symbolic view definitions'{

// template for a custom button *softwareRequirement*:

metadatadefSoftware{// declared metadata definition with the needed attribute 
attributeverificationMethod[0..*] :VerificationMethod; 
} 
packagesoftwareReqTemplate{// custom button template 
#SoftwarerequirementsoftwareRequirement;// the metadata Software is applied to the requirement usage element extending it with a new name Software and a metadata attribute. The name softwareRequirement will be used for the perform action's element specification to pass the template to a button in a button category 
}

// template for a templated button *satisfy*:

packageSatisfyReqTemplate{ 
usecaseuseCase1{ 
satisfyrequirement1; 
} 
requirementrequirement1; 
} 
}

###### Adding custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, specify attributes for the custom templated button:
  1. Redefine the label attribute and specify the button name.
  2. Redefine the description attribute and specify the button's tooltip.
  3. Redefine the representation attribute and specify how the element should be represented in a view. Available *representation* attribute value options:*shape*- regular element symbol as a shape.*shapeOnBorder*- port or other directional feature representation.*path*- relationship element representation.*naryShape*- n-relationship element representation.Note:Relationships can be displayed as paths or n-ary shapes (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) N-ary connection' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Dependency' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) N-ary interface' space='']).Ports or other directional features can be displayed as shapes or shapes on a shape's border.Referential elements can be displayed as shapes or paths (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Perform action' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exhibit state' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Include use case' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Satisfy requirement' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assume constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Require constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assert constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Frame concern' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Occurrences' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views basics' space='']).The attribute can only be applied to *OperationFromTemplate* buttons. The specified representation value is ignored if it is unapplicable to the templated element.
4. Declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in* direction with the custom button's template path using *metadataAccessExpression* . A *metadataAccessExpression* must be used for the *in*element. Its result must be exactly one templated element that you want to copy for the button.Alternatively, you can also use metaclassification expressions, e.g., when the templated element is unnamed. The expression would be: *(**softwareReqTemplate meta KerML::Element).ownedElement*
5. Click the Synchronize button.

package'Custom symbolic view definitions'{ 
privateimportDS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::*; 
viewdef'Requirements View':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ 
part:>>baseViewPalette{ 
partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories{

partsoftwareButton:Button:>abstractButtons{// menu button instance 
attribute:>>label="software requirement";// specify the button name 
attribute:>>description="software requirement";// specify the button tooltip 
// the *representation* attribute not specified because the requirement element can only be represented as a shape 
// *OperationFromTemplate* specifies that the indicated button is templated; in specifies the created element, which is *softwareRequirement* (which also contains metadata) from the template *softwareReqTemplate*: 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>operation{ 
inref=softwareReqTemplate::softwareRequirement.metadata; 
} 
} 
partsatisfyPath:Button:>abstractButtons{ 
attribute:>>label="satisfy path"; 
attribute:>>representation=representation::path;// representation specified as 'path' (*satisfy*can be path or shape) 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{ 
inref= (SatisfyReqTemplate::useCase1metaKerML::Root::Element).ownedElementasSysML::Systems::SatisfyRequirementUsage; 
} 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
