            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying table columns</h3><h4>Modifying inherited table columns</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a869db-c56f-47bc-9fb5-dd9e448a09bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify inherited table columns</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d0f00bde-da9b-4c78-9c4e-693de90e5a62"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To modify an inherited column, redefine the needed column. In its body, modify the column as needed (e.g., hide columns, redefine attributes).</span></li><li data-uuid="057e355e-d998-48c5-8a1d-e8882d395e89"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="726cb49a-0f35-4ef7-bcda-6ec32728347e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view defined by a predefined tabular view:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Custom Tabular View Definitions'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViewDefinition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Qualified Name'</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefining an inherited column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Hiding table columns </span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide table columns </span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3d2f6fa9-564a-407d-8046-14ff6554c40b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the table renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to hide from the diagrammatic representation of the view, and set its multiplicity to zero.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7774048-f217-4a1f-8250-d79c2a561b6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6741f78b-a71e-488e-bf0a-52e2fe17e6ce"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0da79d01-6c4d-4e59-91f4-1ca0e1f8119b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view defined by a predefined tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Custom Tabular View Definitions'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViewDefinition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Owner</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>];</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name</em><em> </em>inherited via <em>Generic Table, </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to hide it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying table column attributes</h3><h4>Modifying table column attributes </h4><p>To modify table column attributes </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ad5a777c-19e0-4b14-a626-3fb532b93088"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, redefine the needed attribute and set its value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3b7ad8d-1fd7-4b30-ad3a-be3609ff40bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Column attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;">Table column attributes predefined in<span> </span><em>DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnView </em>are:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiline</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnPinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to pin the column to the left or right side of the table. Available values: <em>left/right</em>.</span>  </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnSorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order. Available values: <em>asc/desc</em>.</span> </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Integer</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">]; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the <em>sorting </em>attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with <em>sortingOrder</em> set to 0 will take the highest priority.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">width</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Integer</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">]; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to specify the column width. The integer value is interpreted in pixels.</span></span></li></ul><br /><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="54364750-020f-42cb-a60a-93e87e989682"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c36c07e1-96b1-4022-96b4-7659aaf2a599"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Custom Tabular View Definitions'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViewDefinition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Qualified Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting::desc</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in descending order)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (2 - secondary priority)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiline</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// setting multiline to true to wrap a long qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">width</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">200</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the column width</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting::asc</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in ascending order)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (1 - primary priority)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning::left</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// pinning the column to the table's left side</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing table elements display mode</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The table elements' display mode allows you to specify how the exposed elements should be displayed in the table.<span> For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying elements in tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Textual notation</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Attribute <em>DS_Views::CoreViews::'Base Table'::isHierarchical</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4972522f-a188-4565-93ce-45a256516aba"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isHierarchical</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="25c3491f-200e-451d-bb4e-3a85c3047a7c">Shows each element with all of its owners (direct, indirect, and common).</li><li data-uuid="1e32ddb8-1108-42f6-98d5-09728c8167a9">Groups elements under common owners, including those higher up in the ownership hierarchy. </li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Attributes<em> </em><em>DS_Views::CoreViews::'Base Table'::isCompact </em>and<em> isHierarchical</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd6d5b75-731e-420c-a362-514e4b8530f9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute<span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isHierarchical</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>default<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>true<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; isCompact</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="7ffcf65e-b952-42b2-ae40-ab9314816490">Shows each element with its direct owners.</li><li data-uuid="d527eee1-8676-4b09-8ad0-f344be629abf">Groups elements by their common owners when multiple elements share the same one. The omitted indirect owners are part of the common owner path's qualified name, e.g., <em>indirectOwner::commonOwner</em>. </li><li data-uuid="427bd055-cfc7-4372-bcfa-fbb10b3ccc33">Omits indirect owners higher in the hierarchy.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The attributes <em>isHierarchical </em>and <em>isCompact</em> are undefined or set to <em>false</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69e0df23-b340-45ce-9fd4-32248d1d57bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute<span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isHierarchical</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>default<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>false<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; isCompact</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Displays the exposed elements as a plain list.</span></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Managing table elements display mode </h4><p>To manage table elements' display mode </p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="ab80abe9-9da7-45d7-ab14-9f47be4a1061">Within the body of a view definition, do one of the following:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>To set the table elements' display mode to <strong>Complete Tree</strong>:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>Declare an attribute redefining the<span> <em>isHierarchical</em></span><span> </span>attribute and set the default value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></li><li>To set the table elements' display mode to <strong>Compact Tree</strong>:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>Declare an attribute redefining the<span> <em>isHierarchical</em></span><span> </span>attribute and an attribute redefining the<span> <em>isCompact</em></span><span> </span><span>attribute, and set their default values to </span><em>true</em><span>.</span></li></ol></li><li>To set the table elements' display mode to <strong>List</strong>, do any of the following:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>Set the default value for the attribute redefining the <em>isHierarchical </em>or <em>isCompact </em>attribute to <em>false</em>.</li><li>Delete the attributes redefining the <em>isHierarchical</em> or <em>isCompact </em>attributes.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="def5955a-d08f-4dcb-b703-375e2bee1480">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c698415-ef99-4e39-93ab-358bd6501ae2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If <em>isHierarchical </em>is set to <em>true, </em>but <em>isCompact </em>is set to <em>false</em> or is unspecified, the table's display mode is set to <strong>Complete Tree</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27f54811-9b00-42d7-b6a6-606db38097dd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Custom Tabular View Definitions'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViewDefinition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        // sets the table's display mode to 'Compact Tree':</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isHierarchical</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCompact</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Compact tree.png" /></ac:image></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006371 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00042: (2026x Refresh1) Creating Element Creation dialog

- page_id: `304006371`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006371/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+Element+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.127+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model customization > (2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs > (2026x Refresh1) Custom Element Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom Element Creation dialog and submenu creation.

#### NOTE: Updating the Element Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the Element Creation dialog/submenu

Once you have created the new Element Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''].

The default name for the Element Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create Element**. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Element Creation dialog' space=''], the default name remains.

##### Creating a custom Element Creation dialog/submenu

To create a custom Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the ElementCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{// naming the new dialog instance and subclassifying the*ElementCreationDialog* 
} 
}

##### Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

###### Creating element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

To create element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new Element Creation dialog instance, declare a part usage, name it (optional), then subset the itemCategories element.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{ 
partcustomElementsCategory:>itemCategories{// adding a command category 
} 
} 
}

###### Specifying the element creation command category attributes for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

To specify the element creation command category attributes for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{ 
partcustomElementsCategory:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Custom Elements";// naming the element creation command category 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified element creation command category default collapse state 
} 
} 
}

##### Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands

You can customize the Element Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which element creation commands it should contain. The element creation commands are created by targeting one of the following:

- predefined element metaclass . All predefined SysML v2 elements that already exist in the tool can be targeted via the OperationByMetaclass , targeting the element's metaclass.
- custom element template . Any custom-created element can be targeted via the OperationFromTemplate , targeting the corresponding custom-created element templates. For creating custom element templates, see the procedure.

###### Adding element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem and subset abstractItems . This element instantiates the new command.
2. For creating an element creation command for a predefined element:
  1. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the element you want to use for the command using a metadata access expression.
3. For creating an element creation command for a custom element:
  1. Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the label attribute and specifying the command's name.
  2. Ensure a template for the custom element is created. See the procedure.
  3. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the element template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{ 
attribute:>>actionNamedefault"Create Custom Element"; 
partcustomElementsCategory2:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue; 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue; 
attribute:>>labeldefault"SysMLv2 Elements"; 
// element creation command targeting a predefined element via its metaclass: 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new element creation command instance 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass:>>operation{// specifying the element by metaclass that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
in ref= SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage.metadata;// the specified element is a predefined element, which can be targeted by its metaclass 
} 
} 
} 
partcustomElementsCategory1:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse; 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue; 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Custom Elements"; 
 
// element creation command targeting a custom element via its template: 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new element creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"software usage";// specifying the command's name 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the element template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
in ref= (softwareReqTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement;// the specified template is a custom element template, see the procedure 'Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands' 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

###### Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands

To create element templates for custom templated element creation commands

1. Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, do the following:
  1. Declare a metadata definition. In its body, subset annotatedElement and define it by the predefined SysML v2 element used for the customized element.
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] for the specific custom element template. In its body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metadata' space=''] definition to the predefined SysML v2 element used for the customized element and name it.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

package CustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
metadatadefSoftware{ 
:>annotatedElement:SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage; 
} 
packagesoftwareReqTemplate{ // the new template's package, which you will have to target using the *OperationFromTemplate* to create an element creation command for the custom element 
#SoftwarerequirementsoftwareRequirement; 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span>See the following procedures for custom Element Creation dialog and submenu creation.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30292e0a-ed1f-4def-84fb-797ff00dba2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating the Element Creation dialog/submenu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Once you have created the new Element Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activating Element Creation dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63afab54-eb8f-4e0d-bd1b-b2bb3f1b587b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default name for the Element Creation dialog's invocation command is <strong>Create Element</strong>. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming the Create Element command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[set a new name for the command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the default name remains.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating a custom Element Creation dialog/submenu</h3><p>To create a custom Element Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2384b5f2-1ace-4bc6-b051-9e1eb2f36934">In the Containment tree,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Declaring a root namespace"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new root namespace]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="07a5c954-6403-4b30-a367-9fbe2e63e3b5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>for the new namespace.</li><li data-uuid="a33eef10-8445-4226-af05-f945ec2e5979">In the Textual Editor,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>and name it.</li><li data-uuid="6eff0b9a-8525-4f97-a475-7e86f533bf51">Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the <em>ElementCreationDialog</em> definition from the <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs </em>package, then, within the body of the definition:<ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="08d6df2d-b20c-44b1-aefd-e296e6463962"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcc6ce71-0e7d-4eef-b211-eec5504ca6fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomElementCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b891dd78-5409-42bc-a443-9c193cd55723">/ naming the new dialog instance and subclassifying the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b891dd78-5409-42bc-a443-9c193cd55723">ElementCreationDialog</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h3><h4>Creating element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p>To create element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7c89215a-7957-4e8d-8518-19c4ccadd916">Within the body of the part definition element of the new Element Creation dialog instance, declare a part usage, name it (optional), then subset the <em>itemCategories</em> element.</li><li data-uuid="90c00c05-a970-4f4c-a673-6ac055379df1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3c51360-ac52-422f-9f08-5d03243f6bd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomElementCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customElementsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// adding a command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying the element creation command category attributes for the Element Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p>To specify the element creation command category attributes for the Element Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="668d3aba-9887-49e3-98e3-de63b3261ae0">Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="1332909e-5b19-4bd6-b720-414c64c5286c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82af2bdf-5927-4b6c-bf2f-b60511904329"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomElementCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customElementsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Custom Elements&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// naming the element creation command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified element creation command category default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><p>You can customize the Element Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which element creation commands it should contain. The element creation commands are created by targeting one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="add0dcd1-6759-44c7-a6c9-1d06137bddfb"><strong>predefined element metaclass</strong>. All predefined SysML v2 elements that already exist in the tool can be targeted via the <em>OperationByMetaclass</em>, targeting the element's metaclass. </li><li data-uuid="6a5f7e9f-2793-4a42-9ae5-a3e01540e040"><strong>custom element template</strong>. Any custom-created element can be targeted via the <em>OperationFromTemplate</em>, targeting the corresponding custom-created element templates. For creating custom element templates, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.</li></ul><h4>Adding element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8dff9fe4-bd68-4193-a02e-b759ae28fdad">Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem</em> and subset <em>abstractItems</em>. This element instantiates the new command.</li><li data-uuid="c053bffa-1322-4d5e-8947-80958402b9fa">For creating an element creation command for a predefined element:<ol><li data-uuid="77dcc670-a1db-4ff2-9839-81c6ac5c9dc6">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the element you want to use for the command using a metadata access expression.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="fe5e155d-713b-4615-a074-c8143bdacbc7">For creating an element creation command for a custom element:<ol><li data-uuid="b56c8df3-a17e-4c60-89b5-4e6a07e0d7a3">Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the <em>label </em>attribute and specifying the command's name.</li><li data-uuid="fea0d46c-7210-4b96-9279-52a647696dcc">Ensure a template for the custom element is created. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating element templates for custom templated element creation commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.</li><li data-uuid="cac50e6f-efad-4583-9193-81c5602ff2b1">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the element template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="27f52462-9044-46b4-9bd6-bf2a2f75531f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d99a8172-fc34-448c-b498-1aa6696c6e9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomElementCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">actionName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Create Custom Element&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customElementsCategory2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hasVisibleLabels</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;SysMLv2 Elements&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// element creation command targeting a predefined element via its metaclass:</span></u><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">abstractItems</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new element creation command instance</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">operation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the element by metaclass that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage.</span><span element is a predefined element, which can be targeted by its metaclass</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span In its body, <ac:link ac:anchor="Applying metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Metadata" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[apply the metadata]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> definition to the predefined SysML v2 element used for the customized element and name it.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b5b14c35-00e1-4849-b42b-d4abcb0cc57e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6552abf0-5716-48ec-b12c-41d20ff5fc0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomElementCreationDialogs</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Software</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">annotatedElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">softwareReqTemplate</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the new template's package, which you will have to target using the <em>OperationFromTemplate</em> to create an element creation command for the custom element</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Software</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">softwareRequirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005929 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: (2026x Refresh1) Creating parts

- page_id: `304005929`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005929/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+parts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:51.616+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure > (2026x Refresh1) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A part can represent any level of abstraction, different kinds of system components, or a physical component. A system is modeled as a composite part, and its part usages may themselves have further composite structure. A part definition defines a class of part objects, and a part usage is a usage of part or item definitions. Parts may have attributes, ports, and other elements, as well as perform actions or exhibit states.

##### Creating part usage and definition elements

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create part usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *part def*for a part definition.
  2. *part* for a part usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the part's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefFlashlightParts; 
partflashlightSwitch:FlashlightParts;

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create part usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. part def to create a part definition element.
  2. part to create a part usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the part's features.

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create part usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **part** def button to create a part definition element.
  2. The **part** button to create a part usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **part**or the **part def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the part symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the part's features.

###### Creating part usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create part usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the part element.
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the parts compartment, click the Create part button. The new element is created and displayed in the parts compartment.

###### Creating part usage elements via the Specification panel

To create part usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **part**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **parts**compartment section's bar, click the **Create part**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **parts** compartment.

###### Auto-creating part definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create part definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Parts may have attributes, ports, and other elements, as well as perform actions or exhibit states. </p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating part usage and definition elements</h3><h4 <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006379 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: (2026x Refresh1) Creating Relationship Creation dialog

- page_id: `304006379`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006379/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+Relationship+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.342+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model customization > (2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs > (2026x Refresh1) Custom Relationship Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom Relationship Creation dialog and submenu creation.

#### NOTE: Updating the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

Once you have created the new Relationship Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating Relationship Creation dialog' space=''].

The default name for the Relationship Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create Relation**. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Relationship Creation dialog' space=''], the default name remains.

##### Creating a custom Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

To create a custom Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the RelationshipCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomRelationshipCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomRelationshipCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog{ // naming the new dialog instance and subclassifying the *RelationshipCreationDialog* 
} 
}

##### Managing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories

###### Creating relationship creation command categories for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

To create relationship creation command categories for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new Relationship Creation dialog instance, do the following:
2. Create a category for outgoing relations:
  1. Declare a part usage, name it, then subset the itemCategories element. Subsetting the *itemCategories* element instantiates a category for outgoing relations, because the inherited *isIncoming*attribute is undefined. You can reverse it to incoming relations by setting the*isIncoming*attribute to*true*.It is not necessary to have both Outgoing and Incoming categories, as they perform the same function but in opposite directions. You may choose to have only one category.
3. Create a category for incoming relations:
  1. Declare another part usage, name it (optional), then subset the part usage for the outgoing category. In its body, redefine the *isIncoming*attribute, setting it to *true*.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomRelationshipCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomRelationshipCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog{ 
partcustomSourceRelationshipsCategory:>itemCategories{// adding a command category for outgoing relations 
attribute:>>isIncomingdefaultfalse;// may be omitted since the inherited attribute's default value is undefined, resulting in outgoing direction 
} 
partcustomTargetRelationshipsCategory:>itemCategories{// adding a command category for incoming relations 
attribute:>>isIncomingdefaulttrue; 
} 
} 
}

###### Specifying the relationship creation command category attributes for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

To specify the relationship creation command category attributes for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu

1. Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomRelationshipCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomRelationshipCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog{ 
partcustomSourceRelationshipsCategory:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Source"; // naming the relation creation command category 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse; // modified element creation command category default collapse state // the *isIncoming*attribute is undefined, setting the category's commands to outgoing relations 
} 
partcustomTargetRelationshipsCategory:> itemCategories{ 
 attribute :>> label default "Target"; // naming the relation creation command category 
attribute:>>isIncomingdefaulttrue;// setting the category's commands to incoming relations 
} 
} 
}

##### Managing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu commands

You can customize the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which relationship creation commands it should contain. The relationship creation commands are created by targeting one of the following:

- predefined relation metaclass . All predefined SysML v2 relations that already exist in the tool can be targeted via the OperationByMetaclass , targeting the relation's metaclass.
- custom relation template . Any custom-created relations can be targeted via the OperationFromTemplate , targeting the corresponding custom-created relation templates. For creating custom relation templates, see the procedure.

###### Adding relationship creation commands to the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add relationship creation commands to the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem and subset abstractItems . This element instantiates the new command.
2. For creating a relation creation command for a metamodel-defined relation:
  1. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the relation you want to use for the command using a metadata access expression.
3. For creating a relation creation command for a custom relation:
  1. Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the label attribute and specifying the command's name.
  2. Ensure a template for the custom relation is created. See the procedure.
  3. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the relation template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomRelationshipCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomRelationshipCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog{ 
partcustomSourceRelationshipsCategory:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Source"; 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse; 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue;

// relation creation command targeting a metamodel-defined relation via its metaclass: 
partinterfaceUsageItem:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass:>>operation{inref=SysML::Systems::InterfaceUsage.metadata; } 
}

// relation creation command targeting a custom relation via its template: 
partsafetyCriticalConnectionUsageItem:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"safety critical connection"; 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{inref= (safetyCriticalConnectionUsageTemplatemetaKerML::Kernel::Package).ownedElement; } 
} 
} 
} 
}

###### Creating relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands

To create relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands

1. Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] for the custom template.
2. Within the package, specify the custom relation.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomRelationshipCreationDialogs{ 
privateimportScalarValues::*; 
metadatadefSafetyCritical{// the custom element definition 
:>annotatedElement:SysML::ConnectionUsage; 
attributecriticalityLevel:Integer; 
attributerationale:String; 
} 
packagesafetyCriticalConnectionUsageTemplate{// the new template's package, which you will have to target using the OperationFromTemplate to create a relation creation command for the custom element 
#SafetyCriticalconnection; 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span>See the following procedures for custom Relationship Creation dialog and submenu creation.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b245741-8e9c-4ddd-9a2e-967e7783d6c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Once you have created the new Relationship Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activating Relationship Creation dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63afab54-eb8f-4e0d-bd1b-b2bb3f1b587b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default name for the Relationship Creation dialog's invocation command is <strong>Create Relation</strong>. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming the Create Relation command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Relationship Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[set a new name for the command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the default name remains.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating a custom Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</h3><p>To create a custom Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2384b5f2-1ace-4bc6-b051-9e1eb2f36934">In the Containment tree,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Declaring a root namespace"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new root namespace]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="07a5c954-6403-4b30-a367-9fbe2e63e3b5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>for the new namespace.</li><li data-uuid="a33eef10-8445-4226-af05-f945ec2e5979">In the Textual Editor,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>and name it.</li><li data-uuid="6eff0b9a-8525-4f97-a475-7e86f533bf51">Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the <em>RelationshipCreationDialog</em> definition from the <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs </em>package, then, within the body of the definition:<ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><span
````
