Creation dialog/submenu commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="08d6df2d-b20c-44b1-aefd-e296e6463962"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" <em>RelationshipCreationDialog</em></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h3><h4>Creating relationship creation command categories for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p>To create relationship creation command categories for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7c89215a-7957-4e8d-8518-19c4ccadd916">Within the body of the part definition element of the new Relationship Creation dialog instance, do the following:</li><li data-uuid="033932c1-4396-40e0-81e8-d07984be2d04">Create a category for outgoing relations:<ol><li data-uuid="1f8176fa-81e5-4aaa-b26d-dd368c7494a3">Declare a part usage, name it, then subset the <em>itemCategories</em> element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7485c06b-af5f-4459-a1fe-1069c79a77c0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="1ec594dd-ecc6-4aeb-bb7c-7fc8bb35f42f">Subsetting the <em>itemCategories</em> element instantiates a category for outgoing relations, because the inherited <em>isIncoming </em>attribute is undefined. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7f5119c-3070-488c-9925-896832e73dcf">You can reverse it to incoming relations by setting the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7f5119c-3070-488c-9925-896832e73dcf">isIncoming</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7f5119c-3070-488c-9925-896832e73dcf"> attribute to </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7f5119c-3070-488c-9925-896832e73dcf">true</ac:inline-comment-marker></em>. </li><li data-uuid="9cd0e903-68f1-4259-9d24-4eaf4313dd84">It is not necessary to have both Outgoing and Incoming categories, as they perform the same function but in opposite directions. You may choose to have only one category.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="fa6181e6-cae6-40f2-949b-8996a29378d4">Create a category for incoming relations:<ol><li data-uuid="e3f1a3dd-a722-4811-a6cf-59604bf36c22"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="df7846b8-af29-4f62-96cd-f274d21bf0ae">Declare another part usage, name it (optional), then subset the part usage for the outgoing category. In its body, redefine the <em>isIncoming </em>attribute, setting it to <em>true</em>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="90c00c05-a970-4f4c-a673-6ac055379df1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3c51360-ac52-422f-9f08-5d03243f6bd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customSourceRelationshipsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// adding a command category for outgoing relations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isIncoming</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// may be omitted since the inherited attribute's default value is undefined, resulting in outgoing direction</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">         } </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customTargetRelationshipsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// adding a command category for incoming relations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isIncoming</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">         } </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying the relationship creation command category attributes for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p>To specify the relationship creation command category attributes for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="668d3aba-9887-49e3-98e3-de63b3261ae0">Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="1332909e-5b19-4bd6-b720-414c64c5286c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82af2bdf-5927-4b6c-bf2f-b60511904329"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::RelationshipCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customSourceRelationshipsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Source&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// naming the relation creation command category</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified element creation command category default collapse state<br /></span></span>            <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>isIncoming </em>attribute is undefined, setting the category's commands to outgoing relations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customTargetRelationshipsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br />            <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Target&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// naming the relation creation command category</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isIncoming</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// setting the category's commands to incoming relations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><p>You can customize the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which relationship creation commands it should contain. The relationship creation commands are created by targeting one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="add0dcd1-6759-44c7-a6c9-1d06137bddfb"><strong>predefined relation metaclass</strong>. All predefined SysML v2 relations that already exist in the tool can be targeted via the <em>OperationByMetaclass</em>, targeting the relation's metaclass. </li><li data-uuid="6a5f7e9f-2793-4a42-9ae5-a3e01540e040"><strong>custom relation template</strong>. Any custom-created relations can be targeted via the <em>OperationFromTemplate</em>, targeting the corresponding custom-created relation templates. For creating custom relation templates, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.</li></ul><h4>Adding relationship creation commands to the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add relationship creation commands to the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8dff9fe4-bd68-4193-a02e-b759ae28fdad">Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem</em> and subset <em>abstractItems</em>. This element instantiates the new command.</li><li data-uuid="c053bffa-1322-4d5e-8947-80958402b9fa">For creating a relation creation command for a metamodel-defined relation:<ol><li data-uuid="77dcc670-a1db-4ff2-9839-81c6ac5c9dc6">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the relation you want to use for the command using a metadata access expression.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="fe5e155d-713b-4615-a074-c8143bdacbc7">For creating a relation creation command for a custom relation:<ol><li data-uuid="b56c8df3-a17e-4c60-89b5-4e6a07e0d7a3">Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the <em>label </em>attribute and specifying the command's name.</li><li data-uuid="fea0d46c-7210-4b96-9279-52a647696dcc">Ensure a template for the custom relation is created. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating relationship templates for custom templated relationship creation commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.</li><li data-uuid="cac50e6f-efad-4583-9193-81c5602ff2b1">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the relation template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="27f52462-9044-46b4-9bd6-bf2a2f75531f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d99a8172-fc34-448c-b498-1aa6696c6e9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRelationshipCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span custom element</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        # </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">SafetyCritical</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006389 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00045: (2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements

- page_id: `304006389`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006389/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.705+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A requirement is a kind of constraint that specifies stakeholder-imposed constraints that a design solution must satisfy to be a valid solution. There are both requirement definition and requirement usage elements, where a requirement usage is a usage of a requirement definition in some context. The context for multiple requirements can be provided by a package, a part, or another requirement.

- The informal text of a requirement is given by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Annotations' space=''] comments in the body of a requirement definition or usage.
- If a requirement definition or usage is declared with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element names' space=''] , this is also considered its requirement ID .

##### Creating requirement usage and definition elements

###### Creating requirement usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *requirement def*for a requirement definition.
  2. *requirement* for a requirement usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] the requirement in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementdefMassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
doc/* The actual mass shall be less than or equal to the required mass. */ 
attributemassRequired:MassValue; 
}

requirement<'1'>fullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
attribute:>>massRequired=2000[kilogram]; 
}

###### Creating requirement usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. requirement def to create a requirement definition element.
  2. requirement to create a requirement usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] .

###### Creating requirement usage definition elements via the view palette

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **requirement** def button to create a requirement definition element.
  2. The **requirement** button to create a requirement usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **requirement**or the **requirement def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the requirement symbol.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] .

###### Auto-creating requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>requirement def</strong> to create a requirement definition element.</li><li><strong>requirement </strong>to create a requirement usage element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the requirement]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating requirement usage definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create requirement usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>requirement</strong></span> <strong>def </strong>button to create a requirement definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>requirement</strong></span> button to create a requirement usage element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <strong>requirement </strong>or the <strong>requirement def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the requirement symbol.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the requirement]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005473 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: (2026x Refresh1) Creating reusable styles

- page_id: `304005473`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005473/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+reusable+styles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:33.265+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create custom styles that you can later use when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space='']. Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions and/or attribute values allows you to reuse them for the style specification of multiple rules.

Currently, reusable styles can only be created via the Textual Editor.

##### Creating reusable styles

To create reusable style definitions and attribute values

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] in the Textual Editor.
2. Create reusable style attribute values grouped within a custom style class:
  1. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, declare a part usage and specify its name.
  2. Within the body of the part usage, d eclare an attribute, s pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s pecify its value.
3. Create a custom style definition:
  1. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, d eclare a part definition, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition from the DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles package. You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets' space=''] must subclassify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets' space=''] (such as *ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle*, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. 
For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
  2. Within the body of the part definition, d eclare an attribute usage, r edefine the needed attribute, and specify the attribute's value.
4. Reuse the style definitions and/or attribute values when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] . Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] for attributes nested within custom style classes.
5. Synchronize modifications and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet{

// reusable style attribute values grouped within a custom style class: 
partcustomColorPalette{ 
attributecustomDarkGreen:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::Colordefault"#357F35"; 
attributecustomLightGreen:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::Colordefault"#BFF7AA"; 
}

// a custom style definition *PartTextStyleRule*reuses the color specified in the custom style class *customColorPalette:* 
partdefPartTextStyleRule:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle{ 
attribute:>>penColordefaultcustomColorPalette.customDarkGreen; 
} 
// using the custom style definition *PartTextStyleRule*for a rule: 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass=SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
part:>>style:PartTextStyleRule;// the custom style is passed to the rule's style 
} 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005102 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet

- page_id: `304005102`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005102/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+rules+for+a+style+sheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:07.593+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for creating style sheet rules, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] for the created rule.

A rule is created with the *Explicit Elements*condition and *Shape Style*style definition, which includes redefinitions for *fillColor*(with a randomly assigned color value)**and *penColor*(with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. For modifying them as needed, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] pages.

##### Creating custom rules for a style sheet

###### Creating custom rules via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

To create a custom rule via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] .
2. In a view, select the style sheet's part definition element symbol and click the Create Element button on it.
3. In the dropdown menu, click on the rule .
4. You can now:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] .

The element is created in the *rule* compartment.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating custom rules via the Specification panel

To create a custom rule via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] for the style sheet's part definition element.
3. In the Specification panel's **Properties**section bar, click the**Create Element**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
4. In the shortcut menu, click on the **rule**.
5. You can now:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] .

The element is created in the *rule* compartment.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005887 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00048: (2026x Refresh1) Creating states

- page_id: `304005887`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005887/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+states
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:49.791+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

State definitions and usages are used to describe the system’s state-based behavior, where the execution of any particular state is triggered by events. A state usage applies a state definition in various contexts, enabling the reuse of state definitions across different parts of the system.

##### Creating state usage and definition elements

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create state usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *state def*for a state definition.
  2. *state* for a state usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) Specify the state's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

statedefVehicleStates{ 
inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle; 
incontroller:VehicleController; 
} 
statevehicleStates:VehicleStates;

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. state def to create a state definition element.
  2. state to create a state usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the state's features.

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create state usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click one of the following:
  1. The **state** def button to create a state definition element.
  2. The **state** button to create a state usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **state**or the **state def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the state symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the state's features.

###### Auto-creating state definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create state definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating state usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

To auto-create state usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004888 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00049: (2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets

- page_id: `304004888`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004888/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+style+sheets
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:28:14.468+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create a style sheet for custom rule-based symbol styles.

##### Creating a custom style sheet

To create a custom style sheet via the Style Sheets button and apply it to a specific view

1. Open a specific view.
2. Click the Style Sheets button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click the **Create and** Apply Style Sheet command. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
3. (Optional) To reuse existing style sheets, d rag the needed style sheet's part definitionfrom the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the new style sheet'ssymbol, executing the Set Superclassifier command. By reusing an existing style sheet via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subclassification' space=''], you can create custom rules and customize the rules inherited from the reusedstyle sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., *DefaultStyleSheet*) or a custom-created one.For example, you can reuse the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet, then customize itsrulesby changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the*DefaultStyle*Sheet**'s rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the*DefaultStyle*Sheet**.
4. You can now:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a style sheet condition' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] .
    1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] .
    2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] .
  3. Then implement the changes:
    1. Refresh the project to implement the changes by doing one of the following:
      1. P ress the **F5**key on the keyboard.
      2. In the main toolbar, click **View**> **Refresh**.
    2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] .

A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its symbol is displayed in the view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The created style sheet is automatically applied to this view. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004877 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00050: (2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views

- page_id: `304004877`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004877/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+symbolic+views
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:54:29.919+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views' space=''] symbolic views. For basic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Views basics' space=''] page.

##### Creating symbolic views

###### Creating symbolic views via the Create View command

The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:

- Standard predefined symbolic view definitions. See the table on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views' space='']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].

To create symbolic views via the Create View command

1. Right-click an element or element symbol that would be the owner of the new symbolic view.
2. In the shortcut menu, click **Create View**. In the submenu, click the appropriate symbolic view. 
The view is created as a *view usage* element and named after its owner. If the **Create View** command is invoked for:package or namespace - an empty view is created.usage or definition element - a view is created with a docked element symbol representing the element for which the view wascreated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols' space='']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Review the procedures for creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendered]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> symbolic views. For basic view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Views basics" /></ac:link> page.</p><h3>Creating symbolic views </h3><h4>Creating symbolic views via the Create View command</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Standard predefined symbolic view definitions. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>page for a full list.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions" /></ac:link>.</span></li></ul><p>To create symbolic views via the Create View command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click an element or element symbol that would be the owner of the new symbolic view.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create View</strong>. In the submenu, click the appropriate symbolic view.<br />The view is created as a <em>view usage</em> element and named after its owner.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="083da6c2-c870-477d-84c3-5738b9f30bcf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Create View</strong> command is invoked for:</p><ul><li>package or namespace - an empty view is created.</li><li>usage or definition element - a view is created with a docked element symbol <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">representing the element for which the view was </span>created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004959 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00051: (2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views

- page_id: `304004959`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004959/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+tabular+views
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:58:20.912+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views' space=''] tabular views. For basic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Views basics' space=''] page.

##### Creating tabular views

###### Creating tabular views via the Create View command

The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:

- Standard predefined tabular view definitions. See the table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space='']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] .

To create tabular views via the Create View command

1. Right-click an element or element symbol that would be the owner of the new tabular view.
2. In the shortcut menu, click **Create View**. In the submenu, click the appropriate tabular view. 
The view is created as a *view usage* element and named after its owner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Review the procedures for creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendered]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> tabular views. For basic view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Views basics" /></ac:link> page.</p><h3>Creating tabular views </h3><h4>Creating tabular views via the Create View command</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="7e2faee8-f8ea-4dff-b83a-de87fb4356e8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Standard predefined tabular view definitions. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>page for a full list.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="683053fb-343d-4bc9-8fcf-7e00cc4a4ef5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>To create tabular views via the Create View command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click an element or element symbol that would be the owner of the new tabular view.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create View</strong>. In the submenu, click the appropriate tabular view.<br />The view is created as a <em>view usage</em> element and named after its owner.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006102 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: (2026x Refresh1) Creating use cases

- page_id: `304006102`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006102/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+use+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:03.112+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A use case definition specifies the required behavior of its subject relative to one or more external actors. The objective of the use case is to provide an observable result of value to one or more of its actors. The subject and the actors interact in order to achieve this objective. A use case usage is a usage of a use case definition.

##### Creating use case usage and definition elements

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create use case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *use case def*for a use case definition.
  2. use case for a use case usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space=''] in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

usecasedef'Provide Transportation'{ // use case definition 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actordriver:Person; 
actorenvironment:Environment; 
}

use case 'drive vehicle' :'Provide Transportation'; // use case usage

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create use case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. use case def to create a use case definition element.
  2. use case to create a use case usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space=''] .

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create use case usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click one of the following:
  1. The **use case** def button to create a use case definition element.
  2. The **use case** button to create a use case usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **use case**or the **use case def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the use case symbol.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space=''] .

###### Auto-creating use case definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create use case definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating use case usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

To auto-create use case usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006457 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00053: (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites and rules

- page_id: `304006457`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006457/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+validation+suites+and+rules
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:34:00.028+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation > (2026x Refresh1) Custom validation suites and rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for creating validation suites and rules.

#### NOTE: Enabling and updating validation suites

Enabling and updating validation suites

- Once you have created a validation suite, make sure to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enabling validation suites' space=''] .
- If you modify a validation suite or rule, press **F5**or click **View > Refresh**in the main toolbar to see the changes.

##### Creating validation suites and rules

###### Creating a validation suite

To create a validation suite

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the validation suite, subclassify DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite , then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
  3. 
  4. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomValidationSuite{// the new validation suite's package 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{// naming the new validation suite and subclassifying the *DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite* 
} 
}

###### Creating a validation rule

To create a validation rule

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new validation suite, declare a part usage, name it, then subset the templateRule element. The *templateRule*is a reusable template for creating validation rules. It contains all the necessary validation rule components: severity, message, and condition. The template's severity level is set to *error*; to change it, see the procedure.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomValidationSuite{ 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{ 
part'Validate Part Usage Type':>templateRule{// naming a new validation rule and subsetting the *templateRule* 
} 
} 
}

###### Specifying the validation rule severity

For more information on the validation rule severity level, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results' space=''] page.

To specify the validation rule severity

1. Within the body of the part usage element of the new validation rule, declare an attribute usage, redefine severity , and then specify the needed [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomValidationSuite{ 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{ 
part'Validate Part Usage Type':>templateRule{ 
attribute:>>severity=severity::warning;// specifying the validation rule's severity level as *warning* 
} 
} 
}

###### Specifying the validation rule message

To specify the validation rule message

1. Within the body of the part usage element of the new validation rule, declare a part usage and redefine the effect element.
2. Within the body of the effect's part usage, declare an attribute usage, redefine the 'message' element (enclosed in single quotation marks), and specify the message text (enclosed in double quotation marks).
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomValidationSuite{ 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{ 
part'Validate Part Usage Type':>templateRule{ 
attribute:>>severity=severity::warning; 
part:>>effect{// specifying the validation rule's message 
attribute:>>'message'="Part usage must be defined only by part definition."; 
} 
} 
} 
}

###### Specifying the validation rule condition

To specify the validation rule condition

1. Within the body of the part usage element of the new validation rule, declare a part usage and redefine the condition element.
2. Within the body of the condition's part usage, do the following:
  1. (Optional) Declare a ref item , redefine subjectType , and specify the element metaclasses the validation should check. Target metaclasses with metadata access expression. List multiple metaclasses by enclosing them in parentheses and separating them with commas, e.g., *ref item :>> subjectType = (SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::ActionUsage.metadata)*;If you do not specify the *subjectType,* the validation rule applies to all elements and should be narrowed down via the expression specification for the calculation (see the next step).
  2. Declare a calculation usage and redefine the test element. Within the calc's body, specify the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Functions' space=''] for the validation rule's condition. Use functions to construct the condition expression. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Functions' space=''] subpages containing predefined functions with examples and explanations.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

packageCustomValidationSuite{ 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{ 
part'Validate Part Usage Feature Typing':>templateRule{ 
attribute:>>severity=severity::error; 
part:>>effect{ 
attribute:>>'message'="Part usage must be defined only by part definition."; 
} 
// specifying a subjectType, then using a simpler expression in the calculation: 
part:>>condition{ 
refitem:>>subjectType=SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
calc:>>test{ 
(elementasKerML::Feature).typehastypeSysML::Systems::PartDefinition 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

The first option uses the *subjectType to*narrow down evaluable elements to part usages. The calculation's expression: 
*(element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Base Functions' space=''] KerML::Feature).type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Base Functions' space=''] SysML::Systems::PartDefinition* - targets the part usage's type ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Feature typing' space='']), then checks whether it is a part definition. If it is, the condition evaluates to *true* (validation passed); if it is not, the condition evaluates to *false* (validation failed). An element fails the validation rule if the condition expression evaluates to *false*.

50%

packageCustomValidationSuite{ 
partdefCustomValidationSuite:>DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite{ 
part'Validate Part Usage Feature Typing':>templateRule{ 
attribute:>>severity=severity::error; 
part:>>effect{ 
attribute:>>'message'="Part usage must be defined only by part definition."; 
} 
// not specifying a subjectType and using a complete expression in the calculation: 
part:>>condition{ 
calc:>>test{ 
not(elementhastypeSysML::Systems::PartUsage)or(elementasKerML::Feature).typehastypeSysML::Systems::PartDefinition 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

The second option discards *subjectType* and uses an expression to do the same:

*not (element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Base Functions' space=''] SysML::Systems::PartUsage)* - checks if an element is not a part usage. If the element is not a part usage, the condition evaluates to *true* (validation passed); if the element is a part usage, the condition evaluates to *false* (validation failed).

*(element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Base Functions' space=''] KerML::Feature).type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Base Functions' space=''] SysML::Systems::PartDefinition* - targets the part usage's type ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Feature typing' space='']), then checks whether it is a part definition. If it is, the condition evaluates to *true* (validation passed); if it is not, the condition evaluates to *false* (validation failed).

The *or* function checks both conditions; if either one of the conditions, or both, evaluates to *true*, then it returns *true* (validation passed). Thus, if only one of the conditions evaluates to *false*, the outcome is still *true*, meaning the validation rule is not failed and the element passes the validation. The element only fails the validation if it evaluates to *false* for both conditions.

For example, if the evaluated element is:

- a part usage that is defined by a part definition → first condition: ( false ) failed, second condition: ( true ) passed, outcome: validation passed.
- an action usage that is defined by an action definition → first condition: ( true ) passed, because the first condition passed, the second is not evaluated, outcome: validation passed.
- a part usage that is defined by an action definition → first condition: ( false ) failed, second condition: ( false ) failed, outcome: validation failed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
