<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005209 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00058: (2026x Refresh1) Custom Element Creation dialog

- page_id: `304005209`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005209/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+Element+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:22.314+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model customization > (2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Element Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create Element**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element or its symbol in a view.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific elements. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined element creation commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based Element Creation dialog, listing only the element creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the Element Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image on the left shows the Element Creation dialog according to the predefined*SysMLElementCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the custom Element Creation submenu.

##### Element Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create Element command can invoke either an Element Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten element creation commands. Otherwise, the Element Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the element creation commands.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the Element Creation dialog (predefined) and the Element Creation submenu (custom) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the Element Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLElementCreationDialog configuration for the Element Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Creating the Element Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *ElementCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The element dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLElementCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed Element Creation dialog via the **Active Create Element Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00059: (2026x Refresh1) Custom Relationship Creation dialog

- page_id: `304006373`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006373/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+Relationship+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.238+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model customization > (2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Relationship Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create Relation**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element or its symbol in a view.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific relations between elements. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories, typically Incoming and Outgoing.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined relationship creation commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based Relationship Creation dialog, listing only the relationship creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the Relationship Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image on the left shows the Relationship Creation dialog according to the predefined*SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the customized Relationship Creation submenu.

##### Relationship Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create Relation command can invoke either a Relationship Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten relationship creation commands. Otherwise, the Relationship Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the relationship creation commands.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the Relationship Creation dialog (predefined) and the Relationship Creation submenu (custom) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog configuration for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Creating the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *RelationshipCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The relationship dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed Relationship Creation dialog via the **Active Relationship Element Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004843 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00060: (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets

- page_id: `304004843`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004843/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+style+sheets
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:27:13.225+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules.

##### Custom style sheet types

Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. You can create style sheets in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extracting style sheets' space=''] In a view displaying elements with applied metadata, you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. The extracted style sheet is automatically applied to the view and the displayed symbols.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] This option allows you to create a style sheet with your own custom rules.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] This way, you can create custom rules and customize the rules inherited from the reused style sheet.

##### Style sheet composition

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A style sheet is comprised of the following components:

- (Optional) Style sheet condition - specify a condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the style sheet condition. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a style sheet condition' space=''] page.
- Rules - specify conditions and styles for symbols.
  - Condition - specifies to which symbols the rule applies. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] page.
  - Style - specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. You must specify the style definition (e.g., Shape Style ) that comes with predefined attributes (e.g., fill color , font , etc.) available for specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] page.

#### INFO: Style definitions

Style definitions

There are several types of style definitions:

- **Primary style definitions** - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* of the *3DS SysML Customization*library.
  - For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
- **Auxiliary style definitions** - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:
  - **Predefined style definitions** - style definitions specified in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions* package of the *3DS SysML Customization*library (e.g., *AbstractGreenStyle*, *AbstractRedStyle*, etc.). These styles are only used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Predefined style sheets' space=''].
    - For a list of predefined style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
  - **Custom style definitions** - style definitions you create yourself. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating reusable styles' space=''] page.

##### Example

Say you prefer to use the *DefaultStyleSheet,* but you want to modify it so that part usage element symbols display all inherited elements at all times, which is disabled by default in the *DefaultStyleSheet.*

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] for the rule.
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] in the condition compartment by doing the following:
    1. In the Match By dropdown menu, select Element Kind .
    2. Specify the Metaclass field:
      1. In the Containment tab, click Quick Find , and input 'partusage'. In the results section, locate the Part Usage element with the metaclass symbol [ATTACHMENT filename='metaclass icon.png'] . If the element is not displayed, deselect the Apply Filter check box. The element is selected in the Containment tree. [ATTACHMENT filename='quick find.png']
      2. Drag and drop the element from the Containment tree onto the Metaclass field in the Specification panel. The metaclass has been specified as part usage. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying the condition for a style sheet rule.png']
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] in the style attributes compartment by doing the following:
    1. In the Type dropdown menu, select Feature Compartment Style . If you take a look at the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''], you can see that the *showInherited*attribute is available via the *Feature Compartment Style*style definition. You need to specify it for the rule's condition to inherit the *showInherited*attribute*.*For a list of all style definitions, their attributes, value types, and symbols to which they apply, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
    2. In the Owned attributes section, hover over the redefined attributes and click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='delete_button.png'] button. Note: a newly created rule has the Shape Style style definition, which includes redefinitions for fillColor (with a randomly assigned color value) and penColor (with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. Delete the redefining attributes to retain the default style sheet's values for these attributes. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing owned attributes.png']
    3. In the Inherited attributes section, locate the showInherited attribute and click to edit it. Select the check box to set the attribute's value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='modify inherited attribute value.png'] The style attributes compartment looks like this: [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying the style for a style sheet rule.png']
3. Implement the changes:
  1. (Optional) Now, the new style sheet is applied to the view it was created for. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] page.
  2. Refresh the project to implement the changes by doing one of the following:
    1. P ress the **F5**key on the keyboard.
    2. In the main toolbar, click **View**> **Refresh**.
  3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] . The showInherited attribute is one of the layout-affecting attributes, so you need to approve the style changes for affected symbols. See the procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] page. [ATTACHMENT filename='Assessing layout-sensitive changes.png']
  4. The inherited elements can only be displayed in their appropriate compartments. If the compartment is suppressed, display it to see the inherited elements. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page. [ATTACHMENT filename='displaying compartments.png']

The *DefaultStyleSheet* remains applied to the entire project, while the specific view now additionally uses the new style sheet, affecting only part usage symbols; i.e., all of the styles defined in the *DefaultStyleSheet* remain, the only change is that now inherited elements are displayed in part usage symbols.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules. </span></p><h3>Custom style sheet types</h3><p>Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. You can create style sheets in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extracting style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />In a view displaying elements with applied metadata, you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. The extracted style sheet is automatically applied to the view and the displayed symbols. </li><li data-uuid="5d7831d0-408d-4ccd-924f-f60a311f266e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet from scratch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />This option allows you to create a style sheet with your own custom rules.</li><li data-uuid="27b61a22-d8f3-45b1-8ac2-a01677997de6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet by reusing an existing style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">This way, you can create custom rules and customize the rules inherited from the reused </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">style sheet.</span></li></ul><h3>Style sheet composition</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style sheet composition.png" /></ac:image></p><p>A style sheet is comprised of the following components:</p><ul><li data-uuid="25fb58e4-7eff-4cfd-9df7-ffb9d6d1bf39">(Optional) <strong>Style sheet condition </strong>- specify a condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the style sheet condition. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a style sheet condition" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><strong>Rules </strong>- specify conditions and styles for symbols.<strong> </strong><ul><li><strong>Condition </strong>- specifies to which symbols the rule applies. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><strong>Style </strong>- specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. You must specify the style definition (e.g., <em>Shape Style</em>) that comes with predefined attributes (e.g., <em>fill color</em>, <em>font</em>, etc.) available for specification. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="022a23a7-04fb-4c76-b543-22895607abe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Style definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There are several types of style definitions<span>:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Primary style definitions</strong> - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles</em> of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library. </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Auxiliary style definitions</strong> - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Predefined style definitions</strong> - style definitions specified in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions</em> package of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library (e.g., <em>AbstractGreenStyle</em>, <em>AbstractRedStyle</em>, etc.). These styles are only used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a list of predefined style definitions, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Custom style definitions</strong> - style definitions you create yourself. If the element is not displayed, deselect the <strong>Apply Filter </strong>check box. The element is selected in the Containment tree. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick find.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Drag and drop the element from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Metaclass </strong>field in the Specification panel.<br />The metaclass has been specified as part usage.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying the condition for a style sheet rule.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the style for the rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>style attributes </strong>compartment by doing the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Type </strong>dropdown menu, select <em>Feature Compartment Style</em>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6760f1a6-a477-4b50-a128-8cbe42df4a9e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you take a look at the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can see that the <em>showInherited </em>attribute is available via the <em>Feature Compartment Style </em>style definition. You need to specify it for the rule's condition to inherit the <em>showInherited </em>attribute<em>.</em></p><p>For a list of all style definitions, their attributes, value types, and symbols to which they apply, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6f125076-ce2e-4e5b-8b11-5a07175282ea">In the <strong>Owned </strong>attributes section, hover over the redefined attributes and click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button. Note: a newly created rule has the <em>Shape Style </em>style definition, which includes redefinitions for <em>fillColor </em>(with a randomly assigned color value)<em> </em>and <em>penColor </em>(with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. Delete the redefining attributes to retain the default style sheet's values for these attributes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing owned attributes.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="77deaa31-f0cd-4c80-a358-798d6107a06e">In the <strong>Inherited </strong>attributes section, locate the <em>showInherited </em>attribute and click to edit it. Select the check box to set the attribute's value to <em>true</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modify inherited attribute value.png" /></ac:image><br /> The <strong>style attributes </strong>compartment looks like this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying the style for a style sheet rule.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="a476d594-bfc9-44fa-afbc-ab8a705a4fe3">Implement the changes:<ol><li>(Optional) Now, the new style sheet is applied to the view it was created for. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004882 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00061: (2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `304004882`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004882/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:51.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined symbolic view definitions, you can create model-based custom symbolic view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic views with customized palettes.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of a custom symbolic view definition *Requirements View*and a view *Stakeholder Needs*defined by the custom-created view definition*.*

##### Custom symbolic view definition composition

Custom symbolic view definitions differ in the view palettes they may have. A view palette contains button categories, which contain category-specific buttons. Button categories may also contain menu buttons with individual buttons in their submenus. The button categories also have specific attributes specifying the category name, tooltip, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

Creating custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes is enabled by the , which contains all the necessary elements and configurations, allowing you to create your own symbolic view definitions with customized view palettes.

The library also contains the *Base Symbolic View*definition which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom symbolic view definitions. Itcarries the predefined view palette via the *baseViewPalette*part, containing Selection, Tools, and Common button categories. It also carries the rendering feature that indicates how the elements should be displayed in the view.See the .

##### General button categories table

The table below lists the buttons and attributes used in the general button categories - Selection, Tools, Common - used in all predefined symbolic view definitions. These button categories are also used in the *<bsv> 'Base Symbolic View'*via its *baseViewPalette.*You can find the button categories and predefined view palettes in *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*.

| Button categories | Category buttons | Category attributes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SelectionCategory | selectionButtoncompleteCoverageModeButtonselectionMenu:selectSiblingButtonselectOfTheSameTypeButtonselectConnectedButtonselectConnectedRecursivelyButtonselectAllButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Selections"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Selection"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| ToolsCategory | stickyButtonpusherButtonmagnetButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| CommonCategory | textBoxMenu:textBoxButtontextBoxHtmlButtonseparatorMenu:horizontalSeparatorButtonverticalSeparatorButtonrectangularSeparatorButtondocButtoncommentButtonimageButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default true; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default true; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesViews*namespace that enables the custom symbolic view definition creation:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews**
  - **CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.
  - **SymbolicViews**. Contains predefined symbolic views.
  - **SymbolicViewsByExpression**. Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.
  - **TabularViews**. Contains predefined tabular views.
  - **ViewPalettes**. Contains predefined view palettes .
    - **CodeActionIdentifiers**. Contains action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes . The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.
    - **ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined symbolic view definitions, you can create model-based custom symbolic view definitions as part of your projects. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic views with customized palettes.</span></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_kind.png" /></ac:image>An example of a custom symbolic view definition <em>Requirements View </em>and a view <em>Stakeholder Needs </em>defined by the custom-created view definition<em>.</em></h6><h3>Custom symbolic view definition composition</h3><p>Custom symbolic view definitions differ in the view palettes they may have. A view palette contains button categories, which contain category-specific buttons. Button categories may also contain menu buttons with individual buttons in their submenus. The button categories also have specific attributes specifying the category name, tooltip, etc. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_palette_composition.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. <br />The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.   </h6><p><br /></p><p>Creating custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes is enabled by the <ac:link ac:anchor="3DS SysML Customization library structure"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS SysML Customization library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which contains all the necessary elements and configurations, allowing you to create your own symbolic view definitions with <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">customized view palettes.</span></span></p><p>The library also contains the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Base Symbolic View</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> definition which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom symbolic view definitions. It </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">carries the predefined view palette via the <em>baseViewPalette </em>part, containing Selection, Tools, and Common button categories. It also carries the rendering feature that indicates how the elements should be displayed in the view. </span>See the <ac:link ac:anchor="General button categories table" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p><h3>General button categories table</h3><p>The table below lists the buttons and attributes used in the general button categories - Selection, Tools, Common - used in all predefined symbolic view definitions. These button categories are also used in the <em>&lt;bsv&gt; 'Base Symbolic View' </em>via its <em>baseViewPalette. </em>You can find the button categories and predefined view palettes in <em>DS_Views::ViewPalettes</em>. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button categories</th><th>Category buttons</th><th>Category attributes</th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">SelectionCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectionButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">completeCoverageModeButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectionMenu:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectSiblingButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectOfTheSameTypeButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectConnectedButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectConnectedRecursivelyButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectAllButton</span></li></ul></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hasVisibleLabels </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or not</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Selections&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the category name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Selection&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the category tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ToolsCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">stickyButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">pusherButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">magnetButton</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Tools&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Tools&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">CommonCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxMenu:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxHtmlButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">separatorMenu</span>:</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">horizontalSeparatorButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">verticalSeparatorButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rectangularSeparatorButton</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">docButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">commentButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">imageButton</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hasVisibleLabels </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">labels of the contained buttons are</span></span> displayed in the palette or not</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Common&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Common&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em>DassaultSystemesViews </em>namespace that enables the custom symbolic view definition creation:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="6413891a-51cd-4da0-9dca-9a3db52924ae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>&lt;DS_Views&gt; DassaultSystemesViews</strong></span><br /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CoreViews</strong>. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined symbolic views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViewsByExpression</strong>. </span>Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>TabularViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined tabular views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ViewPalettes</strong>. </span>Contains predefined view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CodeActionIdentifiers</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains </span>action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span>. The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation. </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ElementTemplates</strong>. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul>
````
